name: NSF CAREER Webinar
description: The Division of Research invites faculty to attend an upcoming webinar on the NSF Faculty Early Career Development (CAREER) Program, presented by our partner, McAllister & Quinn.

name: Late Horizon Coastal - Highland Interactions Through Ceramic Compositional Analysis in the Lurin Valley, Peru
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 212
description: Join us for another weekly Proseminar Talk: "Late Horizon Coastal - Highland Interactions through Ceramic Compositional Analysis in the Lurin Valley, Peru." Speaker: Gabriela Oré Menéndez

name: Lee Grad Essentials: Elevator Pitch Essentials: Communicating Your Value in 60 Seconds
description: Whether you’re networking, interviewing, or introducing yourself in professional settings, being able to clearly articulate who you are and what you bring to the table is essential. This workshop will guide graduate students through the key elements of an effective elevator pitch, how to tailor it for different audiences, and how to deliver it with confidence. Participants will also have the opportunity to practice and receive feedback.

name: Virtual Grad: Masters of Social Work Information Session
description: Interested in obtaining a Masters of Social Work (MSW)? Join us for an information session! The MSW graduate program informational sessions are designed to provide prospective applicants with valuable information about the social work graduate program. These sessions will thoroughly review the application requirements and offer applicants an opportunity to ask questions they may have about the program and the application process.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: Non-Tenure Track Gatherings (NTTGs)
description: This month’s topic: Discussion with the Ombuds. The UNLV Ombuds, David Schwartz, Ph.D., is spending some time with us to demystify the ombuds office. Please join us for one of the two online sessions to learn about the ombuds office and discuss how we can best address and seek to resolve your NTT issues and concerns. What are NTTGs?

name: Thesis Defense: David Mayser
location:
Greenspun Hall
        Greenspun 5th Floor Dean's Conference Room
description: David Mayser, M.A Candidate Department of Communication Studies Ideology as Lived Rhetoric: Ritual, Perception, and Commemorative Genre in Sport Advisory Committee Members: David R. Gruber, Advisory Committee Chair Emma Bloomfield, Committee Member Tzu-Chiao Chen, Committee Member Ben Burrows, Committee Member

name: Thesis Defense: David Siquiera
description: David Siqueira, M.S Candidate Department of Social Work Collaboration Between Non-Denominational Community-Based Support and Social Work Professionals for Substance-Use Related Family Crises Advisory Committee Members: An-Pyng Sun, Advisory Committee Chair Garrett Pace, Advisory Committee Member Ivet Aldaba, Advisory Committee Member Julian Kilker, Graduate College Representative

name: Yucha Lecture: Advancing Healthy Aging in HIV Through Research
location: Stan Fulton Building - International Gaming Institute
description: Join the School of Nursing for this year’s Yucha Lecture featuring Allison Webel, Ph.D., Associate Dean for Research & Innovation at the University of Washington. From 4–5 p.m., Webel will share her expertise during her talk, “Advancing Healthy Aging in HIV Through Research.” Before the lecture, we invite you to attend our Poster Fair from 1:30–3:30 p.m., showcasing innovative research, educational initiatives, and clinical practice projects from faculty, students, and community partners.

name: Class of 2028 White Coat Ceremony
description: The White Coat Ceremony marks the transition of a medical student from the classroom to the clinic and the hospital bedside. The white coat is a symbol of that transition. The coating of the students by their clinical faculty affirms that the student will continue to put the interests of the patient first. Join Dean Alison Netski and faculty as we celebrate the class of 2028 as they become student physicians entering their clinical clerkship year.

name: UNLV Nursing 2026 Yucha Lecture
location: Stan Fulton Building - International Gaming Institute
description: The UNLV School of Nursing will present the 2026 Yucha Lecture on March 9, 2026, at 4 p.m. inside the Stan Fulton Building. This year’s guest speaker is Allison Webel, Associate Dean for Research and Innovation at the University of Washington School of Nursing. Webel will deliver a keynote presentation titled, “Advancing Healthy Aging in HIV Through Research,” exploring the intersection of chronic disease management and behavioral science.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 235 (Faculty Lounge)
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Intuitive Eating Group at CICFC
location:
Paul McDermott Physical Education - Building A
        Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling
MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Thesis Defense: Shayna Mesko
location:
Shadow Lane Advanced Dental Education
        School of Dental Medicine Building D Classroom 228
description: Shayna Mesko, M.S. Candidate Department of Orthodontics Comparison of Changes in Vertical Cephalometric Measurements Between Invisalign and Fixed Orthodontic Treatment Advisory Committee Members: Dr. James Mah, Advisory Committee Chair Dr. Brian Chrzan, Committee Member Dr. Charles Hill, Committee Member Maxim Gakh, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Graduate College: Inclusion Actions in the Classroom
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 240
description: Creating a learning environment where all students can fully participate and succeed is central to effective teaching. This workshop explores inclusive and equitable teaching strategies that support the diverse learning needs of students in any classroom setting, whether in person or online. Participants will identify practical approaches to fostering a respectful, accessible, and engaging environment that promotes academic success for everyone.

name: Barre to Mocktails: Move, Learn, Connect
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 2015 and Room 1020
description: Join Campus Rec for an empowering afternoon featuring our low-impact Barre class, which incorporates elements of Ballet, Pilates, and resistance training for a full-body workout that will strengthen and challenge you. Afterward, participate in an engaging Alcohol Bystander Training where you'll learn how to recognize the signs of alcohol poisoning, confidently step in when someone’s at risk, and become the supportive bystander who could save a life.

name: Barre to Mocktails: Move, Learn, Connect
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Student Recreation and Wellness Center
Barre Class from 1:00 pm - 1:45 pm in Room 2015
Bystander Training & Mocktails from 2:00 pm - 3:00 pm in Room 1020
description: Join us for an empowering afternoon featuring our low-impact barre class, which incorporates elements of ballet, Pilates, and resistance training for a full-body workout that will strengthen and challenge you. Afterward, participate in an engaging Alcohol Bystander Training where you'll learn how to recognize the signs of alcohol poisoning, confidently step in when someone’s at risk, and become the supportive bystander who could save a life. Both portions of the event can be attended individually—come to one or stay for both!

name: Thriving Authentically – Women’s Professional Panel
location:
Student Union
        Student Union Ballroom A
description: Thriving Authentically is a monthly professional development series designed to foster honest conversations about leadership, identity, and career growth. As part of Women HerStory Month, this special edition centers women’s experiences in professional spaces, highlighting leadership journeys, workplace navigation, and strategies for thriving with authenticity. Attendees will gain practical insight, hear lived experiences from women professionals, and engage in meaningful dialogue focused on long-term personal and professional success. Lunch will be provided.

name: Thesis Defense: Philip Punzalan
location:
Shadow Lane Advanced Dental Education
        School of Dental Medicine Building D Classroom 228
description: Philip Punzalan, M.S. Candidate Department of Orthodontics Correlation Between Maxillary Sinus Volume and Maxillomandibular Size, Position, and Dental Occlusion: A CBCT Retrospective Study Advisory Committee Members: Dr. Sung Kim, Advisory Committee Chair Dr. Brian Chrzan, Advisory Committee Member Dr. Charles Hill, Advisory Committee Member Yonsu Kim, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: FYE: Ready, Set, Register!
description: Need help preparing for summer/fall registration? Meet the First-Year Advising team who are here to help guide you through the process of registering for classes. We will also have the Institute of Financial Literacy and Wellness help you create a plan to pay for those classes. You do not want to miss out on this great opportunity with free pizza and snacks!

name: Richard Shusterman’s Lecture
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building B
        CHB-B216
description: Center for Democratic Culture sponsors a lecture by Richard Shusterman at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. Shusterman is the Dorothy F. Schmidt Eminent Scholar in the Humanities. Educated at Jerusalem and Oxford, he was chair of the Temple University Philosophy Department before coming to FAU in 2005. He has held academic appointments in Paris, Berlin, and Hiroshima and was awarded senior research Fulbright and NEH fellowships.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: Graduate: Virtual Master of Science in Quantitative Finance Information Session
description: Interested in earning a Master of Science in Quantitative Finance at UNLV? Join us to learn how you can complete this rigorous and rewarding degree in just one year. Our faculty and staff will provide insights into the program's structure, curriculum, and the unique opportunities it offers. Discover how this accelerated degree can help you achieve your career goals in the finance industry.

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Admissions Information Session
description: These virtual information sessions are for individuals who are starting to consider medical school as a graduate degree option and career path. We welcome high school seniors, community college and university students, or other individuals just beginning to research medical school and/or beginning to plan for their application. We will offer an overview of the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV, resources to help consider medical school, details regarding application, and a Q&A at the end of the session.

name: On Persian Poetry and the Art of Translation: Celebrating Ahmad Shamlou’s Centennial featuring Niloufar Talebi and Maryam Ala Amjadi
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        Room 101
description: Niloufar Talebi’s new book, a bilingual edition of Ahmad Shamlou, one of Iran’s most influential 20th-century poets, showcases her skill and dedication as a translator and cultural worker. In Elegies of the Earth, published to mark the poet’s centennial, Talebi brings Shamlou’s revolutionary voice of resistance and modernity to English readers.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: International Gaming Investigations
location:
Stan Fulton Building - International Gaming Institute
        Room 220
description: The licensing process is now one of the most important aspects of smartly regulated jurisdictions, ensuring that licensees maintain a culture of compliance.

name: Meet a First Gen Mentor
description: Meet the Intersection's First Gen Mentors. Ask them any questions you might have from students who have been there, and done that. Can't wait to meet you!

name: Property Registration Event
description: Stop by the University Police Department’s property registration table. Whether you’re commuting on two wheels or carrying valuable tech, we welcome students, faculty, and staff to register bicycles, scooters, laptops, tablets, phones, and more. Registering your property helps us assist in recovering your belongings in case of theft or loss. To make your visit quick and easy, please bring the following information for each item you’d like to register: Serial number Model number

name: APIME-FSA Lunch
location:
Lefty-J’s Island Favorites
4550 S Maryland Pkwy Suite D
description: APIME-FSA members are invited to nosh and savor the delectable offerings of Lefty-J’s Island Favorites Restaurant, located across Maryland Parkway in the Runnin’ Rebel Plaza. Offerings include teriyaki chicken, kalua pork, and more!

name: Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Workshop
description: Feeling a little confused about Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) and what it means for your financial aid? No worries, you're not alone! Come to "From Criteria to Clarity: Navigating SAP Standards and the Appeal Process" webinar. We'll break down the SAP policy for keeping your financial aid in simple terms. Plus, we'll show you exactly what to do if you ever need to appeal. Get the info you need to stay on track and keep your aid flowing!

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: High Marks: Robin Goldstein's State Weed Rankings with Special Guest Steve DeAngelo
description: Join the Cannabis Policy Institute on March 11 at 12:30 p.m. for the virtual taping of "High Marks: Robin Goldstein's State Weed Rankings" podcast. Hosts Robin Goldstein, Ph.D., Director of the Cannabis Economics Group at U.C. Davis, and Riana Durrett, Director of the Cannabis Policy institute will welcome special guest, cannabis advocate Steve DeAngelo.

name: RSI Workshop Series: Information & Questions – Providing Information and Responding to Questions
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Providing Information and Responding to Questions. This training will focus on the following outcomes:

name: Recovery Curious
location:
Student Services Complex - Building A
        CAPS Suite 143
description: Are you curious about substance use recovery or ways to reduce use? Join us virtually or in-person to share questions or receive support around the topic of substance use and recovery.

name: Wellness & Service: Assembling Hygiene Kits
description: Join us for a hands-on event where wellness meets community care! The Wellness Zone is teaming up with UNLVolunteers to assemble hygiene kits for those in need—and students who participate will also earn service hours. Helping others through acts of service is a great way to boost your own sense of purpose and well-being. Catering will be provided - First come, first served.

name: Wellness Series: Rebel Refresh
description: Join UNLVolunteers and The Wellness Zone as we sit down and learn about the importance of hygiene and its intersection with food and housing insecurity. We will be building hygiene kits to distribute across campus and in collaboration with a community partner across the Las Vegas Valley.

name: New Employee & Benefits Orientation
description: The university provides a competitive benefits package to benefit eligible employees. During new employee and benefits orientation, we provide an introduction to UNLV and important information about its history and culture; policies and procedures; services and resources available to employees; workplace protection; health, retirement, and voluntary benefits; and more.

name: Virtual: International Student Guide to Graduate School
description: To ensure a smooth transition, the Graduate Recruitment team is here to answer any questions you may inquire regarding your next steps to completing your application at UNLV.

name: Communication Skills Workshop
description: This virtual four-session workshop will provide students with a new set of communication skills each session.

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Calligraphy Workshop
description: In this six-week workshop, students will be introduced to all the essential materials, techniques, and foundational skills needed for Chinese calligraphy. You will learn how to use traditional inkstones and how to maintain your brushes properly. Through guided demonstrations and individual practice, students will develop confidence and control over their brushwork. By the end of the workshop, students will be able to create their own finished calligraphy piece.

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Learn to Canoe
description: Join us in the controlled environment of the SRWC pool for a two-hour class on the basics of canoeing. This course is perfect for first-time canoers or those who want to hone their skills before getting out on open water.

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Dodgeball (A)
location:
Dula Gym 
451 E Bonanza Rd
Las Vegas, NV 89101
description: Get ready for a high-energy, fast-paced competition that puts your teamwork strategy, and reflexes to the test! In this classic team game, players aim to eliminate opponents by hitting them with a dodgeball--while dodging, ducking, and catching their way to victory. If you're hit, you're out.

name: Mindfulness Brief Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note all times are in Eastern Standard.

name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
description: Come and join us in this practice of relaxation as we slowly work our way across various muscle groups to decrease tension and stress. Progressive Muscle Relaxation or PMR, is a relaxation practice developed in the early 1900’s and has repeatedly proven to help with relaxation of the mind and body. Through increasing awareness of what tension feels like, and then using techniques to help your muscles and body relax, the mind follows. PMR has also been shown to support pain relief, better sleep, and other mind-body benefits.

name: Clark County School District: Middle School Choir Festival
description: Middle school choirs from around the valley perform for a distinguished panel of judges.

        MGM Resorts International Patio (outside Rebel Grounds Cafe)
description: You're invited to the third annual Stacks on Stacks Pancake Breakfast, hosted by the UNLV Harrah College of Hospitality in celebration of Rebels Give, UNLV’s annual day of giving. Those who give $10 or more to the College of Hospitality Fund for Excellence are invited to enjoy a pancake breakfast in Hospitality Hall, prepared and served by College of Hospitality faculty, staff, and alumni.

name: Graduate College: Thesis and Dissertation Process: Steps for Formatting Review and Common Mistakes
description: As students complete a thesis, dissertation, or doctoral project, they often focus on the content. Formatting is usually an afterthought, if a thought at all! Yet formatting is critical because your submission cannot be accepted or approved unless formatted properly. Thinking about formatting from the outset can save valuable time, mental bandwidth, and emotional energy near the submission deadline. By attending this workshop, you will learn insider tips and tricks from UNLV’s T/D Formatting Manager, Dr. Jennifer Stevens.

name: Paint Away Stress

name: Narcan Training
description: Join us for a Narcan Training focused on opioid overdose awareness and response. Participants will learn what opioids are, how to recognize the signs of an overdose, and the steps to safely intervene and help save a life if ever needed. This training centers on harm reduction, community care, and building confidence to respond in an emergency. Attendees will receive free Narcan, a CPR mask, and a first aid kit to take home. Chick-fil-A will be provided while supplies last on a first-come, first-served basis.

name: R. Frank & D. Schuman Astronomy Lecture Series: Black Holes at Work
description: Black Holes at Work: How Invisible Monsters Shape Galaxies Travis Fischer, Space Telescope Science Institute in Baltimore Black holes are often portrayed as cosmic vacuum cleaners,but in reality they are powerful engines that shape the galaxies around them. In this talk, Fischer explains what black holes really are, how astronomers detect them despite their invisibility, and how observations with the James Webb Space Telescope allow us to see how black holes drive winds, shocks, and change their host galaxies. Hosted by the UNLV Physics and Astronomy Department.

Yelick, Ph.D., professor and director, Tufts University School of Dental Medicine. Oh will present: "From Sewage to Solutions: Wastewater Intelligence for Community Health in Southern Nevada."

name: Teaching @ UNLV : Applying AI Strategies  in Your Online Teaching Series
description: As part of the Applying AI Strategies in Your Online Teaching series, this session explores the evolving role of AI in teaching and learning. During this 45-minute virtual conversation, faculty will examine common uses of AI in higher education, including research support, brainstorming, and writing, while addressing common misconceptions and practical limitations. By the end of this session you will be able to:

name: REBELPerform Evaluation Training
description: All administrative and classified staff complete their performance evaluations on REBELPerform. This training will provide an overview of the REBELPerform evaluation process. You will learn how to access the evaluation, navigate through the form, add attachments to the evaluation, download evaluations as a PDF, print the evaluations, and more. There will also be a live demonstration and an opportunity to ask questions. This training also serves as a refresher course for those who need additional guidance on how to complete the performance evaluation.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Building Tour - Kerkorian Medical Education Building
description: In 2022, the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV opened its new educational facility in the Las Vegas Medical District. The Office of Admissions is now offering a building tour program for those considering medical education (MD) degree studies.

name: Chinese Ink Painting
description: This 6-week course introduces the fundamentals of traditional Chinese ink painting. Students will explore brush techniques, ink control, and composition while learning to paint classic subjects such as bamboo, flowers, birds, and simple landscapes. Emphasis is placed on expressive brushwork, balance, and the philosophy behind ink painting. Open to beginners and all levels, no prior experience required.

description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: HSI Resource Hub March Webinar: SomosSTEM - A Community-Based, Culturally Engaging STEM Learning Environment at a Rural Hispanic-Serving Institution
description: SomosSTEM is an integrated, community-based, and culturally engaging STEM learning environment at New Mexico Highlands University (NMHU), a public comprehensive Hispanic Serving Institution in Las Vegas, New Mexico. SomosSTEM weaves place-based course-based undergraduate research experiences (CUREs), community internships, near-peer co-designed curriculum (intern-built CUREs), faculty development in culturally responsive mentoring, and sustained partnerships with local agencies into a coherent, longitudinal pathway to promote undergraduate persistence in the life sciences.

name: Responsible Conduct of Research Training
description: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) training is an essential component to make certain that the research activities of UNLV faculty, staff, and students are conducted with integrity and in compliance with regulatory guidance. RCR is defined by The National Institutes of Health (NIH) as the practice of scientific investigation with integrity. It involves the awareness and application of established professional norms and ethical principles in the performance of all activities related to scientific research.

name: Cutting Through the Clutter
description: Overview/Description: Most people have no idea how much their clutter affects them. It can affect their productivity, their self-esteem, their social life and even their weight! People who live with excess clutter say they can’t find the energy to begin to clear it. They constantly feel tired and overwhelmed. When surrounded by clutter, it is impossible to focus or have clarity about life. In this workshop you will learn that clutter is not the result of sloppiness, laziness or incompetence but several surmountable obstacles.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Intuitive Eating Group at CICFC
location:
Paul McDermott Physical Education - Building A
        Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling
MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Virtual: Grad 201 Admissions - Criteria, Context, and Considerations
description: Need help with your grad application? Join us for a workshop to assist with your graduate application. Join us for a workshop on common elements of graduate school applications. In this workshop, we will:

name: REBELPerform Evaluation Training
description: All administrative and classified staff complete their performance evaluations on REBELPerform. This training will provide an overview of the REBELPerform evaluation process. You will learn how to access the evaluation, navigate through the form, add attachments to the evaluation, download evaluations as a PDF, print the evaluations, and more. There will also be a live demonstration and an opportunity to ask questions. This training also serves as a refresher course for those who need additional guidance on how to complete the performance evaluation.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: UNLV World Music Chamber Ensemble: 2026 Lunar New Year Concert
description: directed by Tianxi Wen Join us for the fourth annual UNLV Lunar New Year Concert — an unforgettable evening celebrating the Year of the Horse! This large-scale concert highlights the richness of Asian traditional instrumental and vocal music, powerful kung fu performances, and the elegance of Chinese classical dance — celebrating coexistence, diversity, and cultural exchange.

name: Guided Imagery Practice
description: The mind is a powerful tool! Join us in this brief relaxation exercise as we focus our minds, using our imagination. No experience is necessary. Participants will be briefly educated on what guided imagery is then led through a series of verbal cues and suggestions into their own private place of rest and restoration. Approximately 15 minutes total.

name: Pre Med Mentoring Sessions for 1st Gen and URM Students
description: Mentoring sessions will be available for first generation and underrepresented minority pre-medicine students from UNLV, UNR, NSC, and CSN who are interested in learning about general admissions for the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV. These students will have the opportunity to meet with medical students from the school of medicine, as well as representatives from the school of medicine's Office of Community Affairs.

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: UNLV Background Screening Information and Training Session
description: The information and training session will provide: An overview of policy requirements Implementation guidance Process walkthroughs An opportunity for questions and discussion

name: Communication Skills Workshop
description: This virtual four-session workshop will provide students with a new set of communication skills each session.

name: Calligraphy Workshop
description: In this six-week workshop, students will be introduced to all the essential materials, techniques, and foundational skills needed for Chinese calligraphy. You will learn how to use traditional inkstones and how to maintain your brushes properly. Through guided demonstrations and individual practice, students will develop confidence and control over their brushwork. By the end of the workshop, students will be able to create their own finished calligraphy piece.

name: Lee Business School Alumni Chapter Mixer
location:
Otonomous Hotel
5050 West Russell Rd.
Las Vegas, NV 89118
description: Come mix and mingle with other Lee Business School alumni! Join the UNLV Alumni Association's Business Alumni Chapter for an evening out at the Otonomous Hotel in Spring Valley. Heavy appetizers will be served and the event is open for all. Come for as long as you're able and take advantage of this opportunity to get out and network in a unique setting.Through events and other programming, the Lee Business School Alumni Chapter actively engages alumni throughout the year.

name: Active Assailant 8-Hour Training
location:
CSN Charleston Campus - Building M
        Student Union Room # U126 and U128
description: The University Police Department is pleased to offer a dynamic 8-hour, in-person Active Assailant Training designed to provide hands-on experience and practical tools to help participants prepare for and respond to emergency situations. This comprehensive training will equip participants with: Critical response strategies for active threat situations Interactive, scenario-based exercises that simulate real-life emergencies Expert instruction from law enforcement professionals

location:
Cottage Grove Parking Garage
        Park in the Cottage Grove Garage (charges may apply)
Meet at the benches at the southeast exit of the garage
description: When was the last time you visited UNLV? College of Education alumni are invited back to campus for a custom 60-minute campus golf cart tour. Reminisce about the time you spent here for your studies and activities and check out the newest buildings being developed. Stops at Carlson Education Building, Tam Alumni Center, and the UNLV Bookstore included. Each party will receive a complimentary goodie bag. Space is limited. Up to five attendees per tour allowed.

name: Gratitude Brief Practice
description: Research has shown practicing gratitude leads to a number of mental, physical, and relational benefits; yet it is so easy to be swept up in the seemingly relentless demands of a busy life and feel you don’t have the time to take a moment to consider and appreciate the things you are grateful for. Give yourself the gift of this 10-minute gratitude practice and prioritize your self-care and well-being. Register here for our 10 to 15-minute Gratitude practice!

name: Guided Breathing Practice
description: Revitalize your mind and body in just 15 minutes with alternate nostril breathing. This time-honored technique from Northern India is designed to bring your nervous system into balance, helping you maintain a steady, focused energy throughout the day. Studies show it can help lower blood pressure, improve lung capacity and support circulation. Whether you need an energy boost or a grounding practice to center yourself, this session has you covered.

name: Gratitude Brief Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note all times are in Eastern

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: American Red Cross Lifeguarding Certification Course
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        1210 Wet Classroom
description: The American Red Cross Lifeguarding classes are designed to teach you not only the basics in water safety but also the proper protocol when dealing with any emergencies around water. The Red Cross blends both traditional lecture style and crucial hands-on training in order to teach you effective techniques to deliver prompt care to those in need. After successful completion of the course, participants will be certified in lifeguarding, CPR for the professional rescuer, AED, and first aid.

name: Class of 2026 Match Day Ceremony
location:
Medical Education Building
        MEB 234/235 and Livestreamed on Youtube
description: Please join us to celebrate Match Day for the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine's Class of 2026. Food and entertainment provided as the Class of 2026's residency matches are revealed.

name: Guided Meditation Practice
description: Modern life often keeps us in a constant state of motion—thinking, planning, and responding. Meditation offers a brief but meaningful opportunity to pause, settle the body, and create mental space, even in the middle of a busy day. This Guided Meditation Practice is designed to support relaxation, focus, and emotional balance by gently bringing attention back to the present moment. Through intentional breathing and awareness, participants are invited to release tension, calm the nervous system, and cultivate a sense of steadiness and ease.

name: Canoe Journey to Emerald Cove
description: Go paddling down the Colorado River with Outdoor Adventures! This trip features a 4-mile round-trip canoe journey on the stunning green waters of the Colorado River as we make our way to the famous Emerald Cove. This beginner-friendly paddle is great for both new and experienced canoers—no prior experience required. The trip includes transportation, equipment, instruction, and food.

description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: UNLV Global Success Series: Knowing Your Calories: Reading Food Labels Made Easy
description: Knowing Your Calories: Reading Food Labels Made Easy, will focus on helping individuals understand nutrition labels, including serving sizes, calories, and key nutrients. The goal is to equip the audience with practical skills to make informed and healthier food choices.

location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center

description: The My Professional Origin Story series, hosted by the UNLV Graduate College, highlights the professional journeys of UNLV faculty and administrators. Origin stories matter. Understanding how people came to be where they are can give us insight into where we might be headed or how our own journeys unfold. Appetizers and light refreshments will be provided for in-person attendees.

name: Success Series: Money Matters #1
description: This workshop will cover essential financial topics, including key financial terms, saving money, financing your education, setting financial goals, budgeting, making smart financial decisions after graduation, investing, and planning for major purchases. Equip yourself with the knowledge and skills to confidently navigate your financial journey from student life to your future career. For more information, visit the Student Success Center Coaching page.

name: Nowruz Celebration – Persian New Year
description: Celebrate Nowruz and the Persian New Year as we honor renewal, culture, and community. This program recognizes the arrival of spring and the beginning of a new year through cultural celebration and shared connection. Dinner will be provided. Open to all members of the campus community. More information.

MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Virtual LEE Business Grad School 101 Information Session
description: Find out more about the graduate degree options that the LEE Business School offers. Every student is different. They have varied goals, backgrounds, interests, and these all factor into the degrees they choose. We invite you to explore our programs to find the one that works for you. We provide more than a graduate degree; we provide a graduate student experience. Find out how to be part of the experience during this session.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Perspectives in the Information Ecosystem
description: Whose voices are heard, and whose are not? When is a good time to consult an expert and when is a good time to consult a friend? At its core, research means seeing what other people have to say about your research question and figuring out what that means to you. Learning from others helps you understand your question better. This workshop will explore whose perspectives you can find throughout the information ecosystem, and how to know whose perspective to seek out for a given question.

name: RSI Workshop Series: Direct Instruction – Improving Interaction & Engagement Within Your Courses
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Direct Instruction. This training will focus on the following outcomes:

name: Administrative Faculty March General Meeting
description: Regular monthly meeting to discuss issues of importance to administrative faculty. All administrative faculty are invited to attend online via Zoom. They can also submit items for the agenda anonymously via our New Business for AFC form.

name: Mindfulness Arts and Crafts Corner
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Room 1502 (HED ROOM)
description: A calming place to slow down, breathe, and express yourself through soothing arts and crafts.

name: We Need To Talk About AI
description: Artificial intelligence is reshaping how we teach, learn, create, and govern – but at what cost, and under whose rules? This conversation will examine AI’s expanding role in higher education through the lenses of literacy and access, intellectual property and copyright, environmental impact, and the evolving ethical tensions that define this moment. Join us for a candid, interdisciplinary conversation about how we can embrace AI’s possibilities while thoughtfully addressing its risks.

name: Club Management Association of America - Mock Interviews
description: Practice your Interview Skills with UNLV CMAA! This is a great opportunity to get better and test your interview skills. The mock interview event hosted by UNLV Club Management Association of America helps you practice in a low-pressure setting while receiving valuable feedback. It improves communication skills, boosts confidence, and prepares you for tough questions. By identifying areas for improvement, it enhances professionalism and increases your chances of success in real interviews.

name: Student Veteran and First Generation Study Night in the Library
description: Calling all student veterans and first-generation students! Need help with coursework or looking for a supportive study space? Join us for student veteran and first-generation study night in the library! Drop-in any time during the event to connect with fellow student veterans and first-gen students, access libraries resources, and receive academic support from the libraries. Snacks will be provided. Sponsored by: UNLV Libraries, Rebel Vets, and UNLV First Gen Club.

name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
description: Come and join us in this practice of relaxation as we slowly work our way across various muscle groups to decrease tension and stress. Progressive Muscle Relaxation or PMR, is a relaxation practice developed in the early 1900’s and has repeatedly proven to help with relaxation of the mind and body. Through increasing awareness of what tension feels like, and then using techniques to help your muscles and body relax, the mind follows. PMR has also been shown to support pain relief, better sleep, and other mind-body benefits.

description: Join us at the Lied Library for a chill and productive study session. Study individually or with a group. Coffee, tea, and snacks will be provided!

This session introduces students to using Google Sheets for academic assignments that involve data, charts, or visuals. No prior experience with spreadsheets is required.

name: Beginners' Idea Bridge Workshop Series: Ideation and Prototyping
description: This workshop teaches you how to brainstorm ideas, become comfortable with uncertainty, and prototype minimum viable products and services. You will engage in real time exercises to get them thinking about possibilities and develop ideas into communicable prototypes.

Bring your current work in progress, or stop by for inspiration and conversation. The Libraries' Makerspace will provide material samples and other supplies. Note: Completing a makerspace orientation is not required for these events.

name: Money Talks–SMART Money Moves: Setting Financial Goals That Work
description: Stop wishing and start achieving. This workshop introduces you to the widely recognized SMART goals framework (specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound) and shows you how to apply it directly to your finances. Learn to transform vague aspirations into clear, actionable financial plans for saving, spending, and investing.

name: Pre-Med Veterans Mentoring Session
description: Veteran students and representatives from the Office of Community Affairs at the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV will be available to mentor pre-medicine student veterans from UNLV, NSC, CSN, and UNR who are interested in learning about general admissions for UNLV School of Medicine. Appointments are required.

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session

name: Mind the Gap: Using Sources to Present New Ideas
description: A good research question can’t be answered by consulting just one source! Whether you’re filling a research gap by asking a completely new question or looking at a topic from a new angle, the research process involves grounding your perspective in the context of previous knowledge using a number of different sources. In this interactive workshop, we will explore how to use a combination of sources to support your research claims.

name: Calligraphy Workshop
description: In this six-week workshop, students will be introduced to all the essential materials, techniques, and foundational skills needed for Chinese calligraphy. You will learn how to use traditional inkstones and how to maintain your brushes properly. Through guided demonstrations and individual practice, students will develop confidence and control over their brushwork. By the end of the workshop, students will be able to create their own finished calligraphy piece.

Explore how social media and online platforms can drive powerful change in this interactive session on digital advocacy. Rooted in the Social Change Wheel 2.0, we'll discuss ethical engagement, digital storytelling, and mobilizing communities online. Learn how to amplify your voice and causes you care about in today’s digital age. Build your skills while you snack and scroll for justice.

name: 3D Printing Workshop
description: If you are new to 3D printing this workshop is for you! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. Please be aware that this workshop leads you through the process of 3D printing and time will not be allocated towards your specific project.

name: Campus Conversations for Peace and Civility
description: Join fellow faculty and staff for an interactive virtual session dedicated to strengthening our campus culture. In this online workshop, we will explore practical strategies to cultivate dialogue rooted in peace, mutual respect, and constructive engagement. Moderator José Meléndrez, AVP, Academic Partnerships & Initiatives Opening Remarks Kate Korgan, Ph.D., Acting Executive Vice President & Provost Guest Speakers:

name: Global Thursday: Culture and Conversation
description: Learn about different cultures and share your own, enjoy international snacks, and make new friends every Global Thursday. All UNLV students are welcome!

name: University Forum Lecture: The Fate of the Americas: The Cuban Missile Crisis and the Cold War
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: Renata Keller, associate professor of history at University of Nevada, Reno, will discuss the deadly riots that shook Bolivia when news of the Cuban Missile Crisis broke, the naval quarantine that members of Argentina's armed forces formed around Cuba, the pro-Castro demonstrations organized by Nicaraguan students, and more. This lecture demonstrates that even at the brink of destruction, Latin Americans played active roles in global politics and inter-American relations. Co-sponsored by Tom Wright, Distinguished Emeritus Professor of History, UNLV.

name: Intro To Triathlon - Cycling
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Fitness Studio 1110
description: Join us in preparation for the Triathlon Spring event on April 4. During this class we will guide you through different exercises, techniques and strategies for preparing for the cycling leg of a triathlon. We invite anyone regardless of cycling level or triathlon experience to join us in this free event.

name: Daniel Cox Performs a Doctor of Musical Arts Tuba Recital
location:

        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall

description: Austin Russell’s Junior recital will feature Alexander Felix on percussion. This recital will feature works by Robert Aldridge, Korry Friend, Nicholas Hall, and Brian Mason.

name: Ensuring Integrity: Cybersecurity and AI in the Gambling Sector
description: Cybersecurity breaches in the gambling industry can have wide-reaching and costly consequences. Attacks can lead to gambling platforms being shut down, hackers gaining player account data, and unauthorized access to payment systems, leading to direct revenue loss and regulatory consequences. Cybersecurity is regulated at both the federal and state levels. When responding to incidents, stakeholders must walk a fine line between fairness, integrity, and player safety.

name: Research to Impact: Donuts with the Office of Economic Development
description: Join us and tell us about your research! Drop in for coffee, donuts, and a casual conversation with the Technology Transfer and Industry Engagement teams and tell us about your lab's latest work!

Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Guided Imagery Practice
description: The mind is a powerful tool! Join us in this brief relaxation exercise as we focus our minds, using our imagination. No experience is necessary. Participants will be briefly educated on what guided imagery is then led through a series of verbal cues and suggestions into their own private place of rest and restoration. Approximately 15 minutes total.

name: Mindfulness Brief Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Eastern Standard.

name: Publishing Matters: Digital Accessibility - What Does It Mean for Editors?
description: How do digital accessibility requirements and recommendations change the work of journal editors? Two experts in the scholarly publishing community will discuss ways that journal editors can utilize digital accessibility and expand the potential readership of their publications. Guest Speakers Stacy Scott, Accessibility Officer at Taylor & Francis Lauren Stachew, Senior Digital Publishing Coordinator at University of Michigan Library

name: National AI Literacy Day Event at UNLV
description: On March 27, UNLV will recognize National AI Literacy Day with a practical, campus-wide event focused on strengthening AI literacy in teaching, learning, research, and administrative work. This 90-minute virtual session will introduce the current landscape of AI at UNLV and highlight how different areas of campus are engaging with AI literacy. Through a keynote and a series of lightning talks from colleagues across the university, participants will learn where AI is already showing up in campus practice and where to find resources and support.

name: Dissertation Defense: April Ursula Fox
description: April Ursula Fox, Ph.D. Candidate Department of Educational Psychology, Leadership, & Higher Education Performatic Learning in Informal Education: A Mixed Methods Study of Zine-Making as a Catalyst for Intentional Epistemic Claiming, Embodied Meaning-Making, and Cultural Mediation in a Museum-Based Learning Environment Advisory Committee Members Lisa Bendixen, Advisory Committee Chair Vanessa Vongkulluksn, Advisory Committee Member Jonathan Hilpert, Advisory Committee Member Erin Smith, Advisory Committee Member

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

        Architecture Studies Library
description: This event is free and open to all, including students, alumni, faculty, staff, and the community. The focused fair invites students from all disciplines of the School of Architecture together with representatives from several professional design firms across the AEC industry interested in recruiting summer interns from all year levels or full-time employees from our graduating or graduated classes.

name: Department of Economics Seminar Series: Hale Utar, Grinnell College
description: The Department of Economics would like to invite you to a seminar by Professor Hale Utar from the Grinnell College Utar’s research interests are in international trade and macroeconomics, with a focus on how trade and the macroeconomic environment affect firms and workers.

name: "Conversion to Judaism: A Model for Ethnoracial Identity Change?"
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room 122
description: Amy Reed-Sandoval, Dept. of Philosophy, UNLV—¡Mami sí es mexicana!, my then-four-year-old daughter shouted, tears in her eyes. I was trying to explain that while she and her father are Mexican, I am not. She seemed stunned by my words. Why would I deny all the ways in which Mexican culture is part of our familial life, from the language we speak at home, to the foods we eat, to our relationship to Mexico itself?

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: Interactive Forum: Life After College
description: Join us for an interactive session engaging in meaningful conversations about navigating life after college, exploring career paths, and transitioning into the professional world!

name: BMI Live: Nature is What We Come To in the Practice of Remembering featuring Saretta Morgan with Lazarus Letcher, Erica Vital-Lazare, and Claytee D. White
location:
New West Las Vegas Library
1861 N. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd. Las Vegas, NV 89106
description: This conversation opens a space to celebrate the rich perspectives of Black artists, social change makers, and everyday folks who’ve deepened our capacities to feel, observe, and be present to natural worlds that are often weaponized against us as Black people shaped in various ways by the U.S. South (east/west).

name: Tesura Quartet Chamber Music Concert
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: The Tesura Quartet will perform string quartets by Haydn, Shostakovitch, and Tchaikovsky. Comprised of violinists Sophia Perez-Monroe and Aaron Tseng, violist John Lee, and cellist Matthew Lee, the ensemble was formed in 2024 and has established a reputation as one of the most exciting quartets in the Midwest. Based in Kansas City, the quartet formed out of a passion for music. Since then, the quartet has been selected to participate in festivals, competitions, and masterclasses hosted by some of the country’s leading musicians.

name: UNLVolunteers - Community in Action: The Just One Project
location:
Student Union
        Eating Bar - Under 1st floor stairs
description: Meet us on Friday, March 27 at the Student Union Eating Bar at 8 a.m. for breakfast and conversation before we head out to volunteer with The Just One Project. The Just One Project is a local nonprofit committed to fighting food insecurity and increasing access to food, resources, and essential services through their Food For All and Community Connect programs. Volunteers will assist with assembling grocery bags and boxes of canned and soft goods that will be used for senior home deliveries. Volunteers may also help unload pallets of food and restock warehouse shelves.

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: 2026 Neuroscience Research Showcase
description: Nexus-Neuro: Integrating Science, Medicine, and Society You are invited to participate in the 2026 Spring Neuroscience Research Showcase, a campus-wide symposium highlighting ongoing research in addictions, aging and health disparities, and neuroscience. This event serves as a hub to spark new collaborations across our growing interdisciplinary research communities. The day will feature engaging oral and poster presentations, student presentation awards, and dedicated time for networking.

name: Rebel Red Friday
description: Non-local students and families who are searching for a college are invited to join us for an exclusive day at UNLV! Immerse yourself in our campus life, hear directly from current students, explore our diverse colleges, and discover our housing and dining options. You'll even have the opportunity to fill out an application on the spot.

name: Capture the Flag Competition with C2Society
location:
Thomas T. Beam Engineering Complex - Building A
        Great Hall
description: Join the College of Engineering and the Lee Business School for our spring 2026 Capture the Flag Competition (CTF). This CTF will cover topics including cryptography, forensics, log analysis, OSINT, reverse engineering and more. Prizes for the top scoring team will be given out, so come and give it your best shot! New to CTFs? Don’t worry, all levels are welcome and C2Society will have mentors on hand to assist anyone who may need help.

name: Courageous Dialogue Workshop
description: This event will be a two-hour, in-person program to help participants practice the skills and principles of engaging in meaningful conversation that is open, authentic, and respectful. This event is open to all UNLV students with a current student ID. Students planning to attend should RSVP here on the Involvement Center.

description: Join UNLVolunteers with our semesterly event with the Center for Urban Water Conservation. We will help weed the area and learn about native plants and water conservation. Please RSVP on the Involvement Center.

location:
Wetlands Park
7050 Wetlands Park Lane
Las Vegas, NV 89122
description: Join the Latinx Graduate Student Association, Grad Pride Alliance, and Las Comadres for a walk through Wetlands Park! We'll have snacks, and good conversation about how to navigate graduate school.

name: Sole Hope Shoe Party
location:
Lied Library
        Graduate Student Commons, Room 2141
description: Sole Hope is a nonprofit organization dedicated to combating foot diseases in East Africa, particularly through the prevention of jigger infestations. We will focus on educating participants about jiggers, supporting communities through medical aid, and contributing to job creation in Uganda. Participants will use donated blue jeans to cut out shoe patterns, which will be sent to Uganda for final assembly by local shoemakers.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Table Tennis (A)
location:
Dula Gym
451 E Bonanza Rd
Las Vegas, NV 89101
description: Fast. competitive, and full of energy. the Table Tennis Challenge brings quick reflexes and sharp strategy to the spotlight. Participants will face off in singles or double matches, rallying back and forth in a game that rewards precision, control, and anticipation.

name: Study, Sip & Socialize with Comadres
description: Join us for our monthly writing session created to support your scholarship, your goals, and your community. Whether you’re working on seminar papers, comprehensive exams, articles, your thesis, or dissertation, this is a dedicated space to write with intention alongside fellow comadres. Build momentum, share encouragement, and connect while navigating graduate school together. Come for the focus, stay for the comunidad.

name: Nevada Conservatory Theatre & A Public Fit: Passage
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Black Box Theatre
description: by Christopher Chen a co-production with A Public Fit Theatre Company directed by Ann-Marie Pereth and Joseph D. Kucan

name: An Evening of Masterful Music Featuring Brahms, Gustav Mahler, and Alma Mahler
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Finn Caoile's graduate recital brings together three works from the German Romantic era, united by the artistic relationships of Johannes Brahms and the Mahlers. The program opens with Brahms's Trio in A Minor for Clarinet, Cello, and Piano, opus 114, performed with Chris Armeno on clarinet and Christian Garcia on cello. This is followed by Gustav Mahler's Kindertotenlieder song cycle, performed with Jawan Jenkins, baritone, and concludes with Fünf Lieder by Alma Mahler, performed with Elisabeth Rose Leone, mezzo-soprano.

name: Jonathan Palomar, Mariachi Revolución, & Competition Winners
description: With national mariachi group and vocal competition winners and featured guest artist Jonathan Palomar Immerse yourself in an unforgettable evening of music, culture, and tradition at the 2nd Annual Mariachi Extravaganza—Las Vegas. Witness national award-winning talent from across the nation as the finest mariachi groups and vocalists—winners of the prestigious Mariachi Extravaganza competitions—come together for one spectacular evening.

location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Black Box Theatre

location:
Becker Family Tech
2221 Maverick St
Las Vegas, NV 89108
description: Step into the digital arena for a high-energy competition that blends strategy, teamwork, and fast decision-making. The Esports Challenge brings colleagues together to compete in organized video game matchups designed to test communication, coordination, and quick thinking under pressure.

name: Graduate College: Grad Rebel Writing Boot Camp - Weekend Edition
location:
University Gateway Building
        Gather Rooms 268 and 269
description: Designed for students with weekday commitments, the Grad Rebel Writing Boot Camp: Weekend Edition offers focused support to help you build stronger writing skills and make real progress on your writing projects. This in-person program includes hands-on activities, workshops, and dedicated time for structured writing—all in a supportive, distraction-free environment. The weekend boot camp is hosted at the Graduate College, with breakfast and lunch provided each day.

location: Velvet Vine Wine (511 S Las Vegas Blvd)
description: Your voice belongs in the spotlight! The Jean Nidetch Care Center is proud to sponsor an open mic with Spotlight Poetry as part of the Phenomenal Womxn Poetry Festival. Bring your poetry, spoken word, or personal story to the stage. This open mic centers resilience, healing, and the power of using one’s voice.

name: West Coast Mariachi Group & Vocal Competitions
description: Feel the excitement as talented young artists from across the country compete fiercely for top honors, prizes, and a coveted spot in the prestigious Mariachi Extravaganza concert. From brilliant musicianship to powerful, heartfelt vocals, each performance will highlight the beauty, emotion, and vibrant spirit of the mariachi music tradition.

The weekend boot camp is hosted at the Graduate College, with breakfast and lunch provided each day.

name: Guided Breathing Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note all times are in Eastern Standard.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

description: If you are new to 3D printing this workshop is for you! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. Please be aware that this workshop leads you through the process of 3D printing and time will not be allocated towards your specific project.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

The School of Music offers enriching graduate degree programs for students pursuing careers in composition, conducting, jazz studies, music education, and performance.

name: Lee Grad Essentials: AI in the Workplace
description: This interactive panel discussion will explore how organizations are adopting artificial intelligence tools and what that means for today’s workforce. Industry leaders will share insights on how AI is being integrated into business operations, cybersecurity, and strategic decision-making, as well as what employers expect from graduates entering the field. Panelists will discuss responsible AI use in real-world settings, evolving skill demands, and how professionals are leveraging AI ethically and effectively in real-world environments.

location:

        Jazz Studio, Room 238 (Second Floor)
description: Vocalist Eva Yaz presents an intimate evening of song, featuring Tristan Selzler on piano, Ed Lacala on bass, and Andrés Montero on drums. Her recital highlights the shared spirit of jazz and live music through a personally curated selection of tunes that hold special meaning to Yaz. The program includes:

name: Dress to Express – Gender Affirming Closet Pop-Up
description: Dress to Express is a gender-affirming closet pop-up offering a free thrifting experience in a welcoming and inclusive environment. Participants are invited to explore clothing that supports identity expression and personal style. Refreshments will be provided. In partnership with Runway Rebels, Feminist Club, CAPS, and Gender Care Team. Open to all members of the campus community. More information.

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 235 (Faculty Lounge)
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Challenging Topics in the UNLV Classroom
description: TLC is offering a new reading/discussion group regarding the timely topic of: Challenging Topics in the UNLV Classroom. This Teaching and Learning Commons reading series is brought to you by Beth Barrie, Ph.D., vice provost for Teaching & Learning Innovation. Barrie and members of the TLC team will facilitate discussions. Please note, this conversation might involve difficult subject matter. Thank you for your kind consideration. We are grateful to be part of your important work.

description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

location:
Paul McDermott Physical Education - Building A
        Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling
MPE-A, Room 207
description: The Center for Individual, Couple and Family Counseling will once again be conducting the Intuitive Eating Group in spring 2026. This group provides not only essential psychoeducation, but a judgment-free space to reconnect with your body, while build a healthy, sustainable relationship with food. The group will be held on Tuesdays starting February 10 and will go for 8 weeks. There are two groups: one is online from 12–1 p.m., and one is in-person from 4–5 p.m. weekly.

name: Virtual Meditation Session
description: Pause from your busy schedule and find calm in the noise. Join Nirmala Lekhak, Ph.D., RN, assistant professor at UNLV Nursing, for a virtual guided meditation session from the comfort of your own space, every Tuesday at noon. Meditation increases self-awareness, reduces stress, and can help you connect with the positive energy within. Lekhak guides participants through Maitri Sambodh Dhyaan meditation, a holistic style that combines breathwork, AUM chanting, focused awareness, visualization, and gratitude practice.

name: Be Kind to Your Mind: A Mental Health Panel
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        101
description: We invite you to join us for a mental health panel with real professionals to learn how to take care of yourself and your mind. There will be a portion with written questions and there will be time for open questions from any curious student. Open to all majors.

name: Calvert Award Submission Workshop
description: Interested in submitting your class assignment for a Lance and Elena Calvert Undergraduate Research Award? Come to this drop-in workshop to learn which category might work best for your project, receive tips for putting together a competitive submission, and have your questions answered.

name: Grad Rebel Advantage: Grad Student Panel: Practicing Resilience & Thriving in Grad School
description: Current UNLV graduate students from our Grad Rebel Ambassadors program will share their personal experiences navigating the challenges and triumphs of graduate school. Learn about their approaches for building resilience, maintaining balance, and thriving both academically and personally during grad school.

name: RSI Workshop Series: Facilitation – Facilitating Meaningful Online Discussions
description: In this 45-minute workshop, participants will work on implementing Regular and Substantive Interaction (RSI) in their courses through Feedback. This training will focus on the following outcomes: Explore learning theories behind multimedia learning and its efficacy Identify WebCampus multimedia tools for optimal student engagement Identify appropriate places in your course to utilize multimedia

name: Balancing Act: Cultivating Students' Research Confidence and Intellectual Humility
description: Join the University Libraries for this in-depth discussion on different notions of intellectual humility and how they relate to student research for academic projects and personal questions. We will explore how you as an instructor can encourage intellectual humility without unduly deflating students' confidence in their ability to learn through research. Light refreshments will be provided.

name: "MAKERS" Screening and Panel Discussion
description: The Women's Research Institute is hosting a "MAKERS" Screening and Panel Discussion. The event will feature a 30-minutte episode of the PBS documentary "MAKERS: Women in Nevada," which profiles pioneering women throughout Nevada's history, followed by a panel discussion. Panelists are community organizers, politicians, and special guest of the documentary: *Barbara Buckley: Former Speaker/Nevada Assembly & Executive Director: Legal Aid Center of S. Nevada * Tameka Henry: CCSD Board of Trustees & Founder of Adobo Collective

name: Success Series: Self Care #2
description: In this workshop, you will learn how to prioritize self-care to maintain a healthy balance between your academic and personal responsibilities. This workshop will provide you with strategies and techniques to manage stress, boost your energy, and improve your overall well-being. This workshop will also provide you with tools and strategies to tap into your motivation, develop a growth mindset, and take action towards self-improvement.
