Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Coffee with Karly
description: Join us for Coffee with Karly, a casual and welcoming space to build authentic connections, reflect on your leadership journey, and explore how relationships and mentorship shape who we are as leaders. These monthly conversations offer the opportunity to engage in meaningful dialogue, ask questions, and share experiences that strengthen your leadership skills and sense of community. Through Coffee with Karly, you will:

name: Consent is as Simple as Tea
description: For Sexual Assault Awareness Month (SAAM), the Care Center invites you to Consent is as Simple as Tea. Grab some free boba and engage in an interactive presentation about consent. Through real-life examples and conversation, you’ll explore what consent really means, how it’s communicated, and how to recognize when it’s not present. We’ll also be sharing resources and support available through the Care Center. You’ll also learn more about SAAM, Denim Day, and ways to get involved in this month’s events.

name: Real Talk: What’s in a Dish?
description: Engage in a facilitated dialogue with Mark Padoongpatt, Ph.D., exploring food, identity, and belonging. What to Expect Guided discussion on cultural meaning of food Reflection on identity and lived experiences Opportunities for dialogue and connection This session creates space for meaningful conversation and shared understanding. This event is held in partnership with Interdisciplinary, Gender, and Ethnic Studies

name: The Permanent Residency Process
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        552 or virtual
description: Please join us for an event aimed to answer questions or concerns about applying for UNLV-sponsored permanent residency. The process can seem daunting at times, but Ware Immigration’s Managing Partner Elaine Kimbrell and her team work with ISSC to make the process smoother for international scholars and hiring departments. She will offer us her expertise and advice during her presentation. Please feel free to share this event with any colleagues who are interested in applying for permanent residency through their UNLV department.

name: The Permanent Residency Process
description: Please join us for an event aimed to answer questions or concerns about applying for UNLV-sponsored permanent residency. The process can seem daunting at times, but Ware Immigration’s Managing Partner Elaine Kimbrell and her team work with ISSC to make the process smoother for international scholars and hiring departments. She will offer us her expertise and advice during her presentation. Please feel free to share this event with any colleagues who are interested in applying for permanent residency through their UNLV department.

name: Success Series: Academic Recovery
description: The Success Series at UNLV is a set of virtual workshops and events offered through SAS Coaching that are designed to support students in developing skills and strategies that contribute to their academic success. These workshops are open to all UNLV students and focus on practical topics that help students navigate college challenges, build effective academic habits, and improve overall academic outcomes.

name: Virtual: Grad 101 - Ready for Grad School & How to Apply
description: Considering graduate school? This information session will examine the various personal and professional benefits associated with pursuing graduate education at UNLV. Join us to discuss getting ready for grad school. In this information session we will:

name: Narcan Training
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Health Education Room 1502, 1st Floor of Student Recreation & Wellness Center
description: Join us for a Narcan Training focused on opioid overdose awareness and response. Participants will learn what opioids are, how to recognize the signs of an overdose, and the steps to safely intervene and help save a life if ever needed. This training centers on harm reduction, community care, and building confidence to respond in an emergency. Attendees will receive free Narcan, a CPR mask, and a first aid kit to take home. Chick-fil-A will be provided while supplies last on a first-come, first-served basis.

name: How Should We Study and Teach the American Founding?
description: With the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence approaching, shifting narratives and new civic initiatives raise an important question: How should students study this complex and contested history, and how should teachers teach it? R. Shep Melnick, professor of American politics at Boston College, will give a talk on this subject.

name: Global Thursday: Language Exchange
description: Interested in learning or practicing a new language? Do you love exploring language-culture connections? Join us every second Global Thursday for the Language Exchange Program (LEP) where students can explore languages such as Chinese, German, Vietnamese, Persian, Japanese, and more in a relaxed environment. No prior study required!

name: Special Collections & Archives Open House
description: Are you interested in the history of Las Vegas or UNLV? Ever wondered what happens behind the scenes in Special Collections and Archives? Would you like to learn more about bringing your class in to explore primary sources on the history of the Southern Nevada region? Join us for an open house to learn more about UNLV Libraries Special Collections and Archives and our mission to collect, preserve, and provide access to the history of Las Vegas, Southern Nevada, gaming, and UNLV. You will have a chance to:

name: University Forum Lecture: De-Silencing The Past: Indigenous Resistance And The Decolonization Of Schooling In Canada
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        Room 101
description: Matthew Henderson will offer insight into how both historical analysis and contemporary practice can inform the collective work of reconciliation and educational transformation. This talk will be of particular interest to scholars in the humanities, social sciences, and education, as well as systems leaders and community members dedicated to building schools that are truly responsive to the lands, languages, and peoples they serve, including those seeking to reimagine schooling in contexts such as Nevada and beyond. Co-sponsored by the Department of History.

name: UNLV Life Sciences Career Panel
description: The UNLV School of Life Sciences invites undergraduate and graduate students from all fields to the Life Sciences Career Panel. Arrive as early as 5:30 p.m. for refreshments. Come meet professionals from the Southern Nevada Health District, Federal Drug Administration, and more.

name: Tie Dye the Spring 5k
description: SRWC Fitness presents the second annual Tie Dye the Spring 5k! Come run, walk, roll, skip, dance, or any other fun style you choose to complete the 5k. Wear any color of the rainbow so we can flood campus with color. At the finish line, you will get a race day T-shirt to tie dye in the colors of your choice. Registration to participate is $10 and open to all. Heats run at 7 a.m., 7:30 a.m., and 8 a.m. Please register for the start time you are attending. Join us for a day to move, create, and connect in a colorful way. Registration is now open to all.

name: UNLV Chamber Music Society: Grandeur & Intimacy - Brahms' Piano Quartet, Op. 26
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Recital Hall
description: GET TICKETS TODAY Grandeur and Intimacy features performers Timothy Hoft, piano; Alexander Dzyubinski, violin; Andrew Duckles, viola; and Andrew Smith, cello.

name: Hiring Internationally: Options, Processes, and Concerns (H1Bs and J-1 Scholars)
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        552 or virtual
description: Please join us for an event aimed to answer questions or concerns you may have when it comes to hiring internationally. The process can seem daunting at times, but Ware Immigration’s Managing Partner Elaine Kimbrell and her team work with ISSC to make the process smoother for international scholars and hiring departments. She will offer us her expertise and advice during her presentation. Please feel free to share this event with any colleagues who are involved in international hiring at UNLV.

description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Pacific Standard.

name: Department of Economics Seminar Series: Stefania Garetto, Boston University
description: The Department of Economics would like to invite you to a seminar by Stefania Garetto from Boston University. Garetto’s primary research interests are in international trade and macroeconomics, with a focus on the study of foreign direct investment and multinational firms.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Thesis Defense: Alexandra Castillo Espinoza
location:
John S. Wright Hall - Building B
        Room 114
description: Alexandra Castillo Espinoza, M.A. Candidate A Technical Analysis of Ground Stone From the Group IV Neighborhood at Palenque, Mexico Advisory Committee Members Lisa Johnson, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Barbara Roth, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Ivan Sandoval-Cervantes, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Miriam Melton-Villanueva, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: Publishing Matters: Journal Editors Roundtable
description: Join journal editors, aspiring editors and librarians for a chance to network and problem-solve about changes in the world of scholarly publication. Share ideas for coaching authors, strategies to keep work on schedule, or whatever is bubbling up from your editorial work. Light snacks and drinks will be provided.

name: Addictions iRDA Meeting (In Person and Zoom)
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building B
        Room B425A
description: You are invited to attend the Addictions iRDA meeting in person or online via Zoom. Snacks and drinks will be provided for those attending in person. The Addictions iRDA facilitates interdisciplinary addictions research at UNLV, with the goal of producing high-quality translational research that impacts public health. We aim to foster interdisciplinary collaborations that lead to presentations, publications, new studies, and grant applications.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: Rebel Rumble Bouldering Competition
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Climbing Wall
description: Join us for UNLV's Annual Bouldering Competition: Rebel Rumble! Come join us for the biggest climbing bash of the semester. Challenge yourself and improve your skills in the annual Rebel Rumble Bouldering Competition with divisions in beginner, intermediate, and advanced. Registration is open until the day of. Walk-ins accepted.

name: Engineering Preview
location:
Advanced Engineering Building
        First Floor of AEB
description: Registration is now open for Engineering Preview on Friday, April 17 from 3:30 to 5:30 p.m.

name: American Red Cross Lifeguarding Certification Course
description: The American Red Cross Lifeguarding classes are designed to teach you not only the basics in water safety but also the proper protocol when dealing with any emergencies around water. The Red Cross blends both traditional lecture style and crucial hands-on training in order to teach you effective techniques to deliver prompt care to those in need. After successful completion of the course, participants will be certified in lifeguarding, CPR for the professional rescuer, AED, and first aid.

name: Family Network BBQ at Scarlet & Gray Days
description: We will kick off spring family weekend with a family BBQ! Bring the whole family out for food, lawn games, activities and time together. All guests over the age of 5 must have a $20 ticket. Current UNLV students are free.

name: Clark County School District: Dance Festival & Showcase
description: Dance troupes from around the valley present pieces.

name: School of Journalism and Public Policy Alumni Event
description: The Greenspun College of Urban Affairs will host an alumni open house for graduates of the Hank Greenspun School of Journalism & Media Studies, the Hank Greenspun School of Communication (pre-2007), and Bachelor of Science in Urban Studies graduates from the School of Public Policy and Leadership. Reconnect with classmates and meet faculty and staff.

name: UNLV Baseball vs. Washington State
description: Join us for the UNLV vs Washington State baseball game.

1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: MFA Thesis Exhibition: read the fine print
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Donna Beam Gallery (Room 145)
description: The Donna Beam Gallery presents the MFA Thesis Exhibition of Kayla Lockwood, read the fine print. Lockwood is a multidisciplinary artist whose work interrogates domesticity, memory, and emotional labor, challenging the myth of home as a stable cornerstone of the “American Dream.” She holds a BFA in Art & Technology from the University of Oregon and is currently pursuing her MFA in Art at UNLV.

name: Undergraduate Research Symposium
description: Join the Office of Undergraduate Research (OUR) and the Dr. William W. Sullivan Center for Academic Enrichment and Outreach (CAEO) as we highlight undergraduate student research at our spring 2026 Undergraduate Research Symposium. Undergraduate students will share their work through oral presentations, poster presentations, lightning talks and exhibitions. More information about this event can be accessed on the Research Symposia page.

location:

description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

description: Experience the rich traditions and vibrant culture of Indigenous communities at our Powwow Showcase. This engaging event invites you to learn about and enjoy live performances featuring traditional dance, music, and regalia.

name: Scarlet, Gray & Green Zone
description: The Scarlet, Gray & Green Zone is a dedicated space celebrating sustainability and environmental stewardship. This special area highlights organizations and initiatives that promote conservation, recycling, green living, eco-education, community gardening, and other environmentally focused efforts. Stop by to learn more what UNLV and Southern Nevada has to offer.

name: UNLV Block Party
description: All are invited to the UNLV Block Party, which is part of Scarlet & Gray Days, featuring inflatables, vendors, and music. Come relax, connect, and enjoy a fun-filled event with the UNLV community.

name: UNLV Young Rebels Rally
description: UNLV Young Rebels Rally is a fun-filled community celebration for K-12 students and their families. Join us on campus for arts and crafts, outdoor games, inflatables, face painting, a balloon artist, and refreshments, all while discovering summer camp opportunities available at UNLV. Families will have the chance to explore engaging programs designed to inspire learning, creativity, and college aspirations. Come play, connect, and start planning your summer the Rebel way!

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course

Las Vegas, NV 89131

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: 5K (All teams)
location:
Kellogg Zaher
7901 W Washington Ave
Las Vegas, NV 89128
description: Lace up and hit the course for a test of endurance, speed, and determination! The 5K Challenge invites participants to run, jog, or walk 3.1 miles while supporting their teammates and pushing themselves to achieve personal or team goals.

name: Canoe Journey to Emerald Cove
description: Go paddling down the Colorado River with Outdoor Adventures! This trip features a 4-mile round-trip canoe journey on the stunning green waters of the Colorado River as we make our way to the famous Emerald Cove. This beginner-friendly paddle is great for both new and experienced canoers—no prior experience required. The trip includes transportation, equipment, instruction, and food.

name: 28th Annual Graduate & Professional Student Research Forum
location:
Student Union
        Check-In at the Student Union, 2nd floor, near the SU Ballroom.
Poster Presentations: SU Ballroom
Podium Talk Presentations: SU 219, SU 218, SU 213, SU 211, SU 209, SU 207, SU 205, SU 208C, SU 208B, SU 208A
Creative Expression Presentations: Cohen Theatre (SU 1st floor)
description: Join us for the 28th Annual Graduate & Professional Student Research Forum, a university-wide celebration of graduate and professional scholarship at UNLV. Hosted in collaboration by the Graduate & Professional Student Association (GPSA) and the Graduate College, this event highlights original research, creative projects, and innovation from across all disciplines.

location:
Thomas and Mack Center
        Thomas & Mack Gray Lot
description: UNLV Athletics is proud to continue its biannual Build-A-Bed Project in coordination with Sleep in Heavenly Peace. This year's build is scheduled for Saturday, April 18 beginning at 8 a.m. in the Thomas and Mack parking lot.

name: Grad Admitted Rebel Day
description: Congratulations, you're a Rebel now! Graduate College admitted students and their family members are invited to attend this exclusive experience! During the event, you’ll get the chance to connect with our community and get a glimpse of life as a Rebel! Tour our beautiful campus Check out involvement opportunities Learn about support services Hear about the next steps as an admitted graduate student Learn about graduate financial opportunities

name: Future Formula: Engineering Your K-to-Career Financial Blueprint
location:
Student Services Complex - Building C
        Institute for Financial Literacy and Wellness
description: Join us for Future Formula: Engineering Your K-to-Career Financial Blueprint, a fun, interactive event for K-12 students and families. Learn about college savings options, budgeting strategies, and smart money habits while students enjoy hands-on math and money activities. Enjoy light refreshments, giveaways, and a tour of the newly opened UNLV Institute for Financial Literacy & Wellness. Start building your family’s financial future together!

name: Rebel Preview
description: High school students and their parents/supporters are invited to experience UNLV like never before at our expansive open house event! Whether you're considering applying or have already been admitted, dive into campus life with guided tours and a wide range of informative sessions. Connect with current students, engage with faculty, and explore our campus facilities firsthand. Don't miss this opportunity to envision your future at UNLV – where opportunities abound!

name: Spring Family Weekend: Scarlet & Gray Days
description: UNLV Family Network will celebrate Scarlet & Gray days with the UNLV block party with activities for kids, farmers market, and explore the UNLV campus with academic college open houses. This event is free and open to everyone!

description: TLC is offering a new reading/discussion group regarding the timely topic of: Challenging Topics in the UNLV Classroom. This Teaching and Learning Commons reading series is brought to you by Beth Barrie, Ph.D., vice provost for Teaching & Learning Innovation. Barrie and members of the TLC team will facilitate discussions. Please note, this conversation might involve difficult subject matter. Thank you for your kind consideration. We are grateful to be part of your important work.

name: Teaching @ UNLV – WebCampus Workshop Series: Closing Out the Semester with WebCampus
description: Join Online Education and the Teaching and Learning Commons for a practical, end-of-semester workshop designed to help faculty and graduate assistants wrap up their courses using WebCampus (Canvas). This session will cover best practices for closing out the semester, including using Canvas Analytics to review student engagement, completing the IT end-of-semester checklist, and managing final exams in WebCampus.

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 235 (Faculty Lounge)
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). name: Balancing Work and Life
description: Overview/ Description: Using the philosophy that “those who are happy at home perform better on the job,” this workshop was developed for those who feel burdened with too many responsibilities and without enough time for personal enjoyment. This workshop will help participants explore and define home responsibilities, identify career requirements and prioritize leisure time. The resulting better balance will help achieve more in one’s career and at home.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Yoga Fitness Class
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        SRWC Meeting Room 1010
description: Take a mindful mid-day break with our 30-minute lunch time yoga series, designed specifically for UNLV faculty and staff. These sessions offer a refreshing blend of gentle movement, stretching, and breathwork to help you reset, reduce stress, and return to your day with renewed focus and energy. No experience is necessary—just bring a yoga mat, wear comfortable clothing, and come as you are. ﻿ ﻿Step away from your desk and join us for a well-deserved pause. Your mind and body will thank you!

name: Podcasting 101: Basics of Recording & Editing
description: Join David Ramos Candelas, multimedia and design specialist, to learn the basics of podcast recording and editing using technology found inside of Lied Library's multimedia production studios. During this one-hour workshop, you will explore Lied Library's multimedia resources and practice basic recording and editing.

name: Thriving Authentically
description: Connect with APIME professionals and explore your future career pathway. What to Expect Panel discussion with professionals across industries Insight on navigating career and identity Opportunities to build connections Gain practical advice and learn how to show up authentically in professional spaces. This event is held in partnership with Career Services & Workforce Development.

name: Administrative Faculty April General Meeting
description: Regular monthly meeting to discuss issues of importance to administrative faculty. All administrative faculty are invited to attend in person or online via Zoom. They can also submit items for the agenda anonymously via our New Business for AFC form.

name: Mindfulness Arts and Crafts Corner
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        ROOM 1502 (HED ROOM)
description: A calming place to slow down, breathe, and express yourself through soothing arts and crafts.

name: Undoing & Reclaiming: Filipino Americans and Colonial Mentality
description: Colonial mentality is a unique type of internalized racism in Filipino Americans. This Asian American History Month, join our workshop to learn about Filipino history, the concept of colonial mentality, and how we can challenge it within ourselves!

name: Plan Year 2027 Plan Design and Rate Changes
description: This session is designed to help provide a clear overview of each PEBP health insurance plan offered for the next plan year. We will highlight key differences and review rates, so that you are able to select the option that best fits your family’s needs during open enrollment.

name: Student Veteran and First Generation Study Night in the Library
description: Calling all student veterans and first-generation students! Need help with coursework or looking for a supportive study space? Join us for student veterans and first-generation study night in the library. Drop-in any time during the event to connect with fellow student veterans and first-gen students, access libraries resources, and receive academic support from the libraries. Sponsored by: UNLV Libraries, Rebel Vets, and UNLV First Gen Club.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: Robert D. Faiss Lecture on Gaming Law & Policy with Hon. Cheryl Moss
description: Join us on April 21 for an evening with Honorable Cheryl Moss (ret.) to discuss Betting on Justice: Inside Nevada's Gambling Diversion Court during the annual Robert D. Faiss Lecture on Gaming Law & Policy. Nevada’s Gambling Treatment Diversion Court (GTDC) is the nation’s first and only court designed specifically for individuals whose criminal behavior is driven by disordered gambling. Authorized in 2009 and launched in 2018, it represents a major shift in how a gambling state addresses the intersection of disordered gambling, crime, and accountability.

name: Phi Kappa Phi Annual Induction Ceremony
description: The UNLV chapter of the Honor Society of Phi Kappa Phi, the national’s oldest, largest, and most selective all-discipline academic honor society, will hold its 2026 Induction Ceremony on Tuesday, April 21, in the Student Union Ballroom.

name: Navigating Contemporary Project & Product Management in the Age of AI, Risk, and Regulation
description: Join us for an engaging panel discussion featuring industry leaders who are shaping modern project and product management across high-impact domains. Our panelists bring real-world experience–from product development and delivery, AI implementation programs, PMO leadership and enterprise transformation–and offer practical perspectives on how organizations are delivering value in environments defined by rapid technological change and rising stakeholder expectations.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: Foundations of Casino Surveillance & Investigations
location:
Stan Fulton Building - International Gaming Institute
        220
description: This in-person seminar covers the foundations of casino surveillance, common cheating methods, and a tour of a casino surveillance room. What is the most effective way to monitor a casino floor? Staying one step ahead of advantage players. This comprehensive, hands-on seminar delivers in-depth instruction in casino surveillance, teaching participants precisely what to observe and monitor across some of the world’s most popular casino games.

name: MFA Thesis Exhibition: read the fine print
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Donna Beam Gallery (Room 145)
description: The Donna Beam Gallery presents the MFA Thesis Exhibition of Kayla Lockwood, read the fine print. Lockwood is a multidisciplinary artist whose work interrogates domesticity, memory, and emotional labor, challenging the myth of home as a stable cornerstone of the “American Dream.” She holds a BFA in Art & Technology from the University of Oregon and is currently pursuing her MFA in Art at UNLV.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on November 11, 2025 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026.

name: UNLV Dance: Gravitational Pulls (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on November 18, 2025 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026. Gravitational Pulls features captivating new dance works created by UNLV BFA students in performance & choreography and production & management.

name: UNLV Dance: Orbits (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on October 7, 2025 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026. Orbits is a celestial journey showcasing new works by the esteemed faculty and guest artists of UNLV Dance (including a traditional Georgian dance by choreographer Irakli Rusia) and featuring talented UNLV Dance BFA students.

name: UNLV Dance: Universal Motions (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on March 3, 2026 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026. Universal Motions features choreography by the UNLV Dance faculty, including a reconstruction of a work by 20th century American choreographer, Erick Hawkins.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Art Exhibition: Moth Ball
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Window Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Moth Ball, an installation of a single risograph print in the Museum’s Window Gallery.

name: Art Exhibition: Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Center Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Mourning Songs of Salt and Silt, an exhibition of memorial cyanotypes by Amy Elkins.

name: Art Exhibition: el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking)
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art West Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking), an exhibition of artworks by the Los Angeles-based interdisciplinary artist Jackie Amézquita.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Botanical Prints: A Gelli Printmaking Workshop
description: Celebrate spring in Lied Library's Makerspace as we create foraged art. We will take a walk into the nature of campus and forage simple leaves or flowers, press them onto a Gelli plate, and make our very own botanical prints. All materials for printmaking will be provided. We will be doing a bit of walking so it is recommended that you bring comfortable shoes and a bottle of water.

name: Pen & Paper Palooza
description: Celebrate the art of writing by hand and find your perfect tools! Stop by and Discover: Explore the history of notebooks, writing technology, & the benefits of writing by hand. Pen and Paper Tasting: Test a variety of papers and pens including fountain pens and find what works best for you. Pimp Your Ride: Bring your favorite planner, journal, or notebook and show off your setup.

name: Pre Med Mentoring Sessions for 1st Gen and URM Students
description: Mentoring sessions will be available for first generation and underrepresented minority pre-medicine students from UNLV, UNR, NSC, and CSN who are interested in learning about general admissions for the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV. These students will have the opportunity to meet with medical students from the school of medicine, as well as representatives from the school of medicine's Office of Community Affairs.

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Understanding HSA Accounts
description: HSA Bank, the administrator for both HSAs and HRAs, is offering educational webinars designed specifically for the High Deductible health care participants. Each session provides a clear explanation of the different account type, how they work, and practical tips to help you get the most value from your benefits.

name: Earth Day: Rooting for You
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        Room 101
description: Join the us for a drop-in planting and painting event designed to help you slow down and reconnect. Caring for a plant can be a grounding practice—come paint a pot, plant a succulent, and take home a small reminder to check in with yourself and your well-being.

name: Recovery Curious
location:
Student Services Complex - Building A
        CAPS Suite 143
description: Are you curious about substance use recovery or ways to reduce use? Join us virtually or in-person to share questions or receive support around the topic of substance use and recovery.

name: Impact Awards
description: Join us for the Impact Awards as we celebrate and recognize your philanthropy, community service, and civic engagement over the past year! This event honors the incredible impact students have made both on campus and in the community. Enjoy fun activities, light refreshments, and an afternoon dedicated to recognizing your hard work and commitment to service.

name: "Responses to the Biblical Conquest Tradition"
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room C117
description: Rabbi Brant Rosen, Tzedek Chicago, Chicago, IL— The Biblical commandment to the Israelites to conquer the land of Canaan and annihilate its inhabitants occurs repeatedly in the Torah - and is described in horrifying detail in the book of Joshua.

name: Tai Chi
description: In this six-week program, students will learn foundational Tai Chi movements through guided instruction and gentle practice. Participants will explore posture, breathing techniques, and a sequence of flowing forms designed to improve coordination, flexibility, and mindfulness. Open to beginners and all levels, no prior experience required. Instructor: Alex Woo, Martial Artist and Leader of the Las Vegas Hung Ching Athletic Club.

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Far East Deep South
location:
Student Union
        Philip J. Cohen Theatre
description: Hosted by the Sands Institute for Chinese Language and Culture at UNLV, this special film screening and Q&A features Far East Deep South, a powerful documentary exploring identity, immigration, and the often-overlooked history of Chinese Americans in the segregated South. The film follows Charles Chiu and his family’s journey through the Mississippi Delta as they uncover their ancestral roots and confront the complex realities of race and belonging in America.

name: Game Time
description: Make new friends and improve your English language skills in a relaxed environment while playing fun games. Enjoy snacks and win prizes!

name: Annual President’s Investment Challenge
description: Attend UNLV’s President’s Investment Challenge to see university-wide student teams put their investment skills to the test in a dynamic, real-world competition. Over the course of eight weeks, teams will build and manage investment portfolios, applying financial strategies to achieve top performance.

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: UNLV Community Concert & New Horizons Bands: Concert 2
description: The UNLV Community Concert Band and the UNLV New Horizons Band present their second concert of the 2025-2026 season.

name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Pacific Standard.

name: 2026 Innovation Day
description: Innovation Day is a free campus-wide event showcasing innovative research, startup companies, and translational projects that connect academia, industry, and community partners. It highlights how scientific and technological ideas move beyond the lab into real-world impact and commercialization.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: Plan Year 2027 Plan Design and Rate Changes
description: This session is designed to help provide a clear overview of each PEBP health insurance plan offered for the next plan year. We will highlight key differences and review rates, so that you are able to select the option that best fits your family’s needs during open enrollment.

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Room 112
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Lunch and Learn - Credit: The Relationship You Can't Ghost
location:
Student Services Complex - Building C
        Institute for Financial Literacy and Wellness
description: In the world of "adulting," your credit score is your most important digital reputation. Whether you’re looking to rent your first "big city" apartment or buy a car, your credit wellness is the key to unlocking the door. This workshop covers the essential components of a credit score, how to read the fine print of a credit report, and actionable steps to start building a healthy history right now. Don't ignore the notifications—come learn how to manage the relationship that lasts a lifetime.

name: Understanding HRA Accounts
description: HSA Bank, the administrator for both HSAs and HRAs, is offering educational webinars designed specifically for the High Deductible health care participants. Each session provides a clear explanation of the different account type, how they work, and practical tips to help you get the most value from your benefits.

name: APIME-FSA Community Lunch: Bojangles
description: APIME-FSA members are invited to dine and connect at Bojangles, located at the Harmon Square shopping mall (southwest corner of Harmon Avenue & Paradise Road). Bojangles is a Southern-inspired fast-food chain known for fried chicken & biscuits, plus signature sides.

name: Take Your Child to Work Day: Sunprint Workshop: Making Art with Sunlight
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Lobby
description: Use your imagination. Make pictures with the sun. Children and guardians! Join us at the Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art for Take Your Child to Work Day 2026. You’ll create your own sunprints (also known as cyanotypes) using light-sensitive paper, image templates, and natural or found objects. This free hands-on workshop introduces basic photography concepts and encourages creativity through experimentation with shapes, shadows, and sunlight.

name: Teaching @ UNLV - WebCampus Workshop Series: Closing Out the Semester with WebCampus
description: Join Online Education and the Teaching and Learning Commons for a practical, end-of-semester workshop designed to help faculty and graduate assistants wrap up their courses using WebCampus (Canvas). This session will cover best practices for closing out the semester, including using Canvas Analytics to review student engagement, completing the IT end-of-semester checklist, and managing final exams in WebCampus.

name: Virtual Graduate: Healthcare Administration and Public Health Info. Session
description: Considering a degree in healthcare administration or public health? Join our information session to find out more! We will discuss the benefits of graduate study in the UNLV School of Public Health, as well as the differences between the master of healthcare administration, the executive master of healthcare administration, and the master of public health.

name: Dissertation Defense: James Hines
description: James E Hines, Ph.D. Candidate Department of Educational Psychology, Leadership and Higher Education Student-Athlete Flourishing In Intercollegiate Athletics: A Mixed Methods Study Advisory Committee Members Federick Ngo, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Stefani Relles, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Lisa Bendixen, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Chia-Liang (Leo) Dai, Ph.D., CHES, Graduate College Representative

name: Tai Chi and Football
description: Hosted by the Sands Institute for Chinese Language and Culture at UNLV, and co-hosted by Chinese Corner, this special lecture features guest speaker Baldwin Chiu, who brings over three decades of martial arts experience to explore the connection between Tai Chi and athletic performance.

name: 3D Printing Workshop
description: If you are new to 3D printing this workshop is for you! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. Please be aware that this workshop leads you through the process of 3D printing and time will not be allocated towards your specific project.

name: 5th Annual President's Innovation Challenge
location:
Thomas and Mack Center
        StripView Pavilion
description: Attend UNLV’s 5th Annual President’s Innovation Challenge to witness multidisciplinary teams of students present groundbreaking solutions that use artificial intelligence to re-imagine the future of human-centric industries and solve the most pressing challenges facing dynamic communities like Las Vegas and beyond.

name: Global Thursday: Culture and Conversation
description: Learn about different cultures and share your own, enjoy international snacks, and make new friends every Global Thursday. All UNLV students are welcome!

name: University Forum Lecture: The Growing Divide In American Jewish Community Over Israel/Palestine
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: Rabbi Brant Rosen will examine this important Jewish communal shift, including the increasing involvement of antizionist Jews in the Palestine solidarity movement, the growth of organizations such as Jewish Voice for Peace and #IfNotNow and the beginning of American Jews who are creating Jewish ritual spaces that affirm a Judaism beyond Zionism. Co-sponsored by the Departments of Philosophy, Political Science, Anthropology, Sociology, IGES, History, and English

name: Virtual: Master of Science in Hotel Administration Information Session
description: Interested in hotel administration? Join us to learn more about what the Master of Hotel Administration at UNLV has to offer! The 31-hour Master of Science in Hotel Administration degree program will prepare you for a successful career as an upper-level executive in the hospitality industry. The typical completion time for full-time students is 1 year; part-time students usually take 2 years to complete the program.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Walk Race (All Teams)
location:
Kellogg Zaher
7901 W Washington Ave
Las Vegas, NV 89128
description: Step up to a fun, active, and team-friendly competition! The Walk Race Challenge encourages participants to cover a set distance at a brisk pace, testing endurance, strategy, and determination while supporting teammates along the route.

name: Rebels After Dark
description: Join REB for our late night event of the semester! Free food and activities for all UNLV students.

name: UNLV Symphonic Winds in Concert 2
description: The UNLV Symphonic Winds perform in their second concert of the 2025-2026 season.

name: Take Your Child To Work Day
description: UNLV will celebrate Take Your Child to Work Day on Thursday, April 23, hosted by the Women’s Council. The event invites UNLV employees to bring their children to campus to participate in a variety of activities showcasing UNLV’s diverse offerings. For some, this is their first time on a college campus, making it a great opportunity to inspire their future goals.

name: GeoSymposium
location:
Science and Engineering Building
        Lobby and Auditorium
description: The Department of Geoscience is pleased to host the 21st Annual GeoSymposium – the culminating research and networking event for the academic year at which Geoscience students to present their research to industry partners, alumni, faculty, staff and fellow students. Keynote Addresses Camille Calimlim Touton, '06 Former Commissioner, U.S. Bureau of Reclamation Katie Chakhova, Permitting Specialist, Barrick Mining Corporation

name: Nursing: Final Examination Ends
description: Final exams end for UNLV Nursing. Also marks the end of the Spring 2026 semester.

name: Las Vegas World Intellectual Property Day: Intellectual Property and Sports
description: Join the UNLV Libraries Patent & Trademark Resource Center powered by Switch for a dynamic celebration of World Intellectual Property Day, where innovation meets competition. This year’s theme explores how intellectual property fuels creativity, branding, and global impact across the world of sports, from the business of major leagues to the legal frameworks that protect athletes, teams, and ideas.

location:
Student Union
        First Floor Eating Bar
description: DASH is one of the longest-running, key signature programs here at UNLV. Join UNLVolunteers as we build peanut butter & jelly sandwiches lunch bags to hand out during the community lunch hour at the Salvation Army! We will be meeting at the Student Union 1st floor, near the eating bar at 11:15 a.m. to begin our meal prep shift.

name: Virtual: Master of Arts - Urban Leadership Information Session
description: Please join us to review the Master of Arts Urban Leadership! Aimed at actively engaged working professionals, this degree is offered online to maximize student flexibility, with optional in-person experiential opportunities available.

description: Travis Hammond, Ph.D. Candidate Goals, Properly Technical: Special Education Teachers Using ChatGPT to Develop Individualized Student Goals Advisory Committee Members: Joseph Morgan, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Heather VanNess, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Alex Smith, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Kendall Hartley, Ph.D.,Graduate College Representative

location:
Student Union
        First Floor Eating Bar
description: DASH is one of the longest-running, key signature programs here at UNLV. Join UNLVolunteers as we build peanut butter & jelly sandwiches lunch bags to hand out during the community lunch hour at the Salvation Army! We will be meeting at the Student Union 1st floor, near the eating bar. Volunteers will participate in a short educational activity followed immediately by preparing lunch bags to hand out at the Salvation Army.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Zion Horizons: Camping Weekend
description: Join us for an incredible three-day adventure as we explore the world-renowned Zion National Park! Over the course of the trip, we’ll hike some of Zion’s most iconic trails—including the Emerald Pools—as well as discover hidden gems and less-traveled routes within the park. Evenings will be spent around the campfire with warm food, s’mores, and plenty of time to connect with fellow students under the stars. The trip includes transportation, equipment, instruction, and food.

name: Dissertation Defense: Analiesa Delgado
location:
John S. Wright Hall - Building B
        326
description: Analiesa Delgado, Ph.D. Candidate History Department Fortifying Kinship: California Indian Children’s Resilience at the Fort Bidwell and Greenville Indian Schools, 1884–1928 Advisory Committee Members: W. Bauer, Ph.D., Committee Chair M. Winkelmann, Ph.D., Committee Member D. Clemente, Ph.D., Committee Member M. Tusan, Ph.D., Committee Member S. Johnson, Ph.D., Committee Member S. Sexton, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Depression 101: Mood Boosting Tips and Tricks
description: Learn about depression and practical strategies to improve your mood!

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: Artist Talk: Jackie Amézquita
description: Jackie Amézquita brings contemporary art together with the forces of la tierra, the earth that supports and nurtures us. Join us for her artist talk on April 24 to find out how she created a practice that embraces both active physical contact with the landscape and monumental artworks that can be experienced within the confines of a gallery. What does it mean to move between places? How has she used the experience of migration to talk about her past, her future?

name: Nevada ACDA: All-State Choirs Concert
description: Choir students from around the state of Nevada perform in this spectacular event. The Middle School Choir conductor is Andy Beck. Beck is the Director of Choral Publications at Alfred music and a highly sought-after guest conductor, clinician, and adjudicator. A prolific choral arranger with over 450 popular published works, Beck has also published several music education texts and resources materials.

name: Trivia Night
description: You are invited to Trivia Night hosted by the Indian Students Association–Maitri! Join us for an evening of fun, friendly competition, and exciting trivia. Bring your friends, test your knowledge, and compete for bragging rights. Whether you’re a trivia expert or just coming for the fun, everyone is welcome! Light refreshments will be provided. We hope to see you there for a great night of laughter and teamwork.

name: UNLV Choral Ensembles: HOME Concert - Bearing Witness
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Recital Hall
description: Music by Antonín Dvořák and Alex Berko featuring UNLV Singers Joseph Svendsen, conductor Artem Alexanyan, keyboard

name: UNLV Dance: Elegant Universes
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Dance Studio One (Room 111)
description: Elegant Universes is a polished showcase of new dance works created by UNLV BFA students in performance & choreography and production & management.

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: MFA Thesis Exhibition: read the fine print
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Donna Beam Gallery (Room 145)
description: The Donna Beam Gallery presents the MFA Thesis Exhibition of Kayla Lockwood, read the fine print. Lockwood is a multidisciplinary artist whose work interrogates domesticity, memory, and emotional labor, challenging the myth of home as a stable cornerstone of the “American Dream.” She holds a BFA in Art & Technology from the University of Oregon and is currently pursuing her MFA in Art at UNLV.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art West Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present el Camino se Traza al Andar (the Path is Traced by Walking), an exhibition of artworks by the Los Angeles-based interdisciplinary artist Jackie Amézquita.

name: American Red Cross CPR/AED/First Aid Certification Course
description: The Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED course incorporates the latest science and teaches students to recognize and care for a variety of first aid emergencies such as burns, cuts, scrapes, sudden illnesses, head, neck, back injuries, heat and cold emergencies and how to respond to breathing and cardiac emergencies to help victims of any age - adults (about 12 years and older) and pediatric (infants and children up to 12 years of age). Students who successfully complete this course will receive a certificate for adult and pediatric first aid/CPR/AED valid for two years.

name: Student Dialogue and Brunch: Mind, Community and Connection
description: This event creates a safe and inclusive space for African students and diaspora members in Las Vegas to come together, share experiences, and build meaningful connections. Centered on the theme Mind, Community, and Connection, the session will explore mental health challenges among students and highlight practical ways to navigate them. Through open dialogue, networking, and expert insights, participants will leave feeling empowered, supported, and more connected to their community.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Kickball (A)
description: Step onto the field for a fun, fast-paced game that blends teamwork, strategy, and friendly competition! In the Kickball Challenge, teams take turns kicking the ball and running bases while defending their side to prevent opponents from scoring.

name: Ronald Reagan Debate Series: Las Vegas Regional Invitational
description: High school students are invited to join an exciting debate experience featuring a unique format designed for all skill levels. This opportunity is perfect for students interested in performing arts, communication, leadership, decision-making, and civics. Even better? Participants can compete for up to $50,000 in national scholarships and the chance to win an all-expenses-paid trip to California. Step up, share your voice, and see where your ideas can take you! Prizes for Top Debaters:

name: APIME-FSA Farmer’s Market Meetup at Uncommons
description: APIME-FSA members and their families are invited to connect at the Las Vegas Farmers Market at UnCommmons. This community event features an eclectic mix of fresh seasonal produce, handcrafted gifts, fresh-baked goods, locally made sauces and more. While the event is scheduled 10am-2pm, APIME-FSA members are gathering between 10 -11:30 a.m.

name: Family Swim at SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during this time. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: LaVell Thompson, Jr. Vocal Lecture Recital
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Recital Hall, room HFA 147 (first floor)
description: LaVell will present his final recital as a graduate student in the School of Music at UNLV. The lecture will be comprised of research from his recently completed dissertation entitled Negro Spirituals: The Long Journey West. In alignment with the dissertation's thesis, spirituals will be presented, including two new arrangements by Thompson! He will be joined by some friends and colleagues who will also present one selection of a choral number.

name: The Grey Area: A Junior Recital
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: The Grey Area is a joint junior recital by Kalani Parra Molina, double bass, and Alejandro Arias, classical guitar, highlighting the beauty and nuance of both the classical and jazz styles of music while also acknowledging the space in between.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Horseshoes (A)
description: Test your precision, strategy, and focus in this classic outdoor game! The Horseshoes Challenge invites participants to toss horseshoes toward a stake, aiming for a ringer or the closest landing to score points.

name: Free Billie Eilish 3D Sneak Preview KUNV-FM Screening – May 4 at AMC Town Square
location:
AMC Town Square 
6587 Las Vegas Blvd S
Las Vegas, NV 89119
description: 91.5 KUNV, UNLV’s award-winning radio station, is giving away free tickets to a special sneak preview of Billie Eilish: Hit Me Hard and Soft—presented in immersive 3D. Date: Monday, May 4 Time: 7 p.m. Location: AMC Town Square, Las Vegas KUNV listeners and the UNLV community are invited to experience this exclusive screening before its official release.

name: War, Politics, and Biology: a 'Big Historians' View of Past Phenomena
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 212
description: Please join the Department of Anthropology for the final Spring 2026 proseminar speaker series: Title: War, Politics, and Biology: A ‘Big Historians’ View of Past Phenomena Speaker: Brian Villmoare, Ph.D., Department of Anthropology UNLV

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        404
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response. Audience

name: 3D Printing Workshop
description: If you are new to 3D printing this workshop is for you! This workshop will cover how to find and download 3D printable files, how to convert them to a g-code file that the printer can read, and how to start one of our 3D printers. Please be aware that this workshop leads you through the process of 3D printing and time will not be allocated towards your specific project.

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

name: Global Grads & Friends: End-of-Semester Coffee Meetup
description: Join us for a special end-of-semester event as we bring together graduate and international students to celebrate the close of another successful term. Taking place on April 27 at 2 p.m. at the Student Union Starbucks, this event is designed to create a warm and welcoming space for connection and community.

name: Success Series: Finishing Strong
description: In this workshop, you will learn how to prepare for the final weeks of the semester and finish the semester with confidence. This workshop will cover strategies for effectively reviewing course material, managing your time, and reducing stress. For more information, visit the Academic Success Center Coaching page.

name: Paúl Loja Prado Performs Artist Diploma Flute Recital
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Paúl Loja Prado, flute, presents his final Artist Diploma recital as part of his studies at UNLV. This program features a diverse selection of works for flute, showcasing both standard repertoire and twentieth-century music.

name: Spring Jazz Fest: Harmon Ave. Jazz Vocal Ensemble & Jazz Ensemble III
description: The Harmon Avenue Vocal Jazz Ensembles and Jazz Ensembles III perform in the first night of UNLV's spring jazz festival.

name: Yoonji Park Doctor of Musical Arts Chamber Recital
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Pianist and doctor of musical arts candidate Yoonji Park performs works by Beethoven, Brahms, and Clara Schumann with violin and cello. Parking

description: Stop by the Tam Alumni Center or the Registrar’s Office to pick up your Countdown to Commencement wristband and exclusive swag! Your wristband will give you access to special events and deals happening throughout the week.

name: Guided Imagery Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Pacific Standard.

name: It's Just Coffee: Partisanship in American Politics
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups (approximately once a month) for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning.

name: Sikolohiyang Pilipino: Filipino Psychology
description: Have you ever heard of utang na loob? Walang hiya? Kapwa? Sikolohiyang Pilipino, or Filipino Psychology, has concepts rooted in indigenous practices and values that better capture the Filipino experience. Join us this Asian American History Month in an online workshop to learn more about its history and foundational concepts!

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Understanding HSA Accounts
description: HSA Bank, the administrator for both HSAs and HRAs, is offering educational webinars designed specifically for the High Deductible health care participants. Each session provides a clear explanation of the different account type, how they work, and practical tips to help you get the most value from your benefits.

name: Between the Lines: Grad Cap Decorating & Candy Lei Event
description: Graduating this winter or know someone who is? Join us to decorate your graduation cap and make a candy lei for yourself or a friend! This event is all about celebrating your hard work and the journey that got you here—through creativity, connection, and care. Taking time to reflect and create can support emotional wellness during life transitions like graduation. All supplies provided—just bring your ideas and good vibes

name: Leis & Caps: Celebrate Our Grads
description: Celebrate this milestone with a meaningful and creative experience. What to Expect Graduation cap decorating Lei making and gifting Space to celebrate and reflect Honor your journey and celebrate alongside community. This event is held in partnership with UNLV Wellness Zone.

name: Non-Thesis Defense: Olivia Perez
description: Olivia Perez - M.S. Non-Thesis Defense Master of Science - Kinesiology Examining Sex-Specific Validity in Algorithmic Estimates of Exercise Energy Expenditure From Commercial Wrist-Worn Wearable Devices Department of Kinesiology and Nutrition Sciences James Navalta, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair John Mercer, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Michael Wong, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Nancy Lough, Ed.D.Graduate College Representative

name: My Professional Origin Story - Satish C. Bhatnagar, Ph.D.
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: The My Professional Origin Story series, hosted by the UNLV Graduate College, highlights the professional journeys of UNLV faculty and administrators. Origin stories matter. Understanding how people came to be where they are can give us insight into where we might be headed or how our own journeys unfold. Appetizers and light refreshments will be provided for in-person attendees.

name: 2026 Rebel Awards
description: Join us for the 2026 Rebel Awards Ceremony as we celebrate the nominees and winners of this year’s student awards.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Sport Tuesday
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Meet at 4:45 PM at the SRWC Lobby
description: Sports Tuesday is all about staying active and making fitness fun with your UNLV international community! Each week, we join workout classes, do cardio together, or play sports! Meet in the lobby of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center at 4:45 PM.

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Admissions Information Session
description: These virtual information sessions are for individuals who are starting to consider medical school as a graduate degree option and career path. We welcome high school seniors, community college and university students, or other individuals just beginning to research medical school and/or beginning to plan for their application. We will offer an overview of the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV, resources to help consider medical school, details regarding application, and a Q&A at the end of the session.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Cornhole (A)
description: Bring your aim, strategy, and friendly competition to the table (or board!) in this classic backyard game. In the Cornhole Challenge, teams take turns tossing beanbags toward the target board, aiming to land in the hole or score points by landing on the board.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: Oboist Nicholas Carozza Performs a Lecture Recital
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: In this lecture recital, doctor of musical arts candidate Nicholas Carozza will present a historical background and analysis of Noël for voice, oboe, and piano composed in 1901 by Clémence de Grandval (1828–1907). The work was Grandval’s eleventh and final song using the poetry of Nobel laureate Sully Prudhomme and was dedicated to oboist Louis Bleuzet and baritone Paul Daraux. Parking

name: Spring Jazz Fest: Jazz Ensemble I + Studio Scarlet Jazz Vocal & Latin Jazz Ensembles
description: Studio Scarlet Jazz Vocal Ensemble, the Latin Jazz Ensemble, and Jazz Ensemble I perform in the second concert of UNLV's spring jazz festival.
