name: Countdown to Commencement Wristband & Swag Pick Up
location:
Richard Tam Alumni Center
        Tam Alumni Center and Registrar's Office
description: Stop by the Tam Alumni Center or the Registrar’s Office to pick up your Countdown to Commencement wristband and exclusive swag! Your wristband will give you access to special events and deals happening throughout the week.

name: Denim Day Workshop Series
description: Join us for a full day of connection, reflection, and community as we honor Denim Day on April 29. The Care Center is hosting a series of free activities throughout the day to support victim-survivors while promoting education, prevention, and a safer campus. Learn more about the meaning behind Denim Day and take part in spaces designed for healing, creativity, and conversation. Itinerary:

name: UNLV Lifelong Learning's Open House
description: The UNLV campus community is invited to the UNLV Lifelong Learning open house. Meet the team, explore programs, and gain insight into how offerings can support your professional and personal goals. You’ll also learn how UNLV Lifelong Learning can serve as a strategic partner for your department, staff, or students through workforce development and personal enrichment opportunities.

name: Countdown to Commencement Workshops
description: Join us for workshops geared to undergraduate and graduate college graduates.

name: Keep Calm and Carry Yarn
description: Yarn crafters and yarn-craft-curious folks are invited to join a Keep Calm and Carry Yarn gathering in the Lied Library lobby. We invite thread and embroidery floss crafters to join us! Bring your current work in progress, or stop by for inspiration and conversation. The Libraries' Makerspace will provide material samples and other supplies. Completing a makerspace orientation is not required for these events.

name: Money Talks - Your Future Wealth: A Casual Lunch & Learn on Investing
location:
Student Services Complex - Building C
        Institute for Financial Literacy and Wellness
description: You don’t need to be a Wall Street insider to start building your financial future—you just need the right information. Join experts from the UNLV Finance Department and J.P. Morgan Chase for a casual lunch and learn covering the basics of investing in plain English. Grab some free food, bring your biggest questions, and learn how to make your money work for you—whether you have $50 to invest or are planning for the long term!

name: Pre-Med Veterans Mentoring Session
description: Veteran students and representatives from the Office of Community Affairs at the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV will be available to mentor pre-medicine student veterans from UNLV, NSC, CSN, and UNR who are interested in learning about general admissions for UNLV School of Medicine. Appointments are required.

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Plan Year 2027 Plan Design and Rate Changes
description: This session is designed to help provide a clear overview of each PEBP health insurance plan offered for the next plan year. We will highlight key differences and review rates, so that you are able to select the option that best fits your family’s needs during open enrollment.

name: Virtual: Grad 201 Admissions - Criteria, Context, and Considerations
description: Need help with your grad application? Join us for a workshop to assist with your graduate application. Join us for a workshop on common elements of graduate school applications. In this workshop, we will:

name: Virtual: International Student Guide to Graduate School
description: To ensure a smooth transition, the Graduate Recruitment team is here to answer any questions you may inquire regarding your next steps to completing your application at UNLV.

name: Dissertation Defense: Anthony Graham
location:
John S. Wright Hall - Building B
        Room 326
description: Anthony Graham, Ph. D. Candidate Department of History Inventing The Mojave: Boosterism, Visual Culture, And Perceptions Of Deserts In The Intermountain West. Advisory Committee Members: Andy Kirk, Advisory Committee Chair Michael Green, Advisory Committee Member Deirdre Clemente, Advisory Committee Member Robert Futrell, Graduate College Representative

name: 2026 Academic Achievement Award Ceremony
description: UNLV and the Nevada System of Higher Education (NSHE) Board of Regents recognize the achievements of faculty in teaching, research/creative activity, and service with a number of awards at the annual Academic Achievement Awards Ceremony. Achievements of undergraduate and graduate advisors and undergraduate and graduate scholars are also recognized. A brief reception follows.

name: Tai Chi
description: In this six-week program, students will learn foundational Tai Chi movements through guided instruction and gentle practice. Participants will explore posture, breathing techniques, and a sequence of flowing forms designed to improve coordination, flexibility, and mindfulness. Open to beginners and all levels, no prior experience required. Instructor: Alex Woo, Martial Artist and Leader of the Las Vegas Hung Ching Athletic Club.

name: Robert E. Lang Memorial Fellowship Lecture - Celene Fuller
description: The Robert E. Lang Memorial Fellowship is awarded annually to support an outstanding UNLV Ph.D. student completing a dissertation in metropolitan public policy broadly defined. At the end of their fellowship year, fellows present their doctoral research in a public forum. The 2025 -26 Robert E. Lang Memorial Fellow, Celene Fuller, will present a lecture titled "Abortion Politics and Policy: Stigma and Access in the Silver State."

name: End of Semester Social with the Latinx Graduate Student Association
location:
Student Union
        Sunset Park (by the model boat ramp)
2601 E Sunset Rd
Las Vegas, Nevada
description: Join the Latinx Graduate Student Association for our end of semester celebration. We will have snacks, refreshments, and good conversation as we celebrate everyone's accomplishments.

name: FYE - Rebels Rising Up Day
description: Join the FYE Team to celebrate the finale of your First-Year Experience (FYE) at UNLV. This event will include a chance to reflect and celebrate the completion of your first year - see you there!

name: Taste the Beat: Happy APIME Heritage Month!
description: Experience a food-centered celebration highlighting bold flavors from across the Asian diaspora. What to Expect A variety of culturally inspired dishes Opportunities to explore new flavors A dynamic dining experience Held in partnership with UNLV Eats.

name: Senior Sunrise with CSUN
description: Senior Sunrise with CSUN is a celebratory event for all graduating seniors from the class of 2026, including both spring and fall graduates. This event provides an opportunity for students to come together, reflect on their time at UNLV, and celebrate their accomplishments as they prepare to graduate. Attendees can enjoy a sunrise gathering with music, photo opportunities, exclusive merchandise, a coffee truck, breakfast burritos, and more.

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: From City Hall to Capitol Hill: Strengthening Advocacy Across All Levels
description: This training will equip participants with the knowledge to effectively advocate across all levels of government, local, county, state, and federal. Participants will learn how advocacy strategies differ at each level, and how to identify key decision-makers and understand their roles in shaping public policy.

name: Spring Jazz Fest: Jazz Ensemble II + Maryland Pkwy. Jazz Vocal & Contemporary Jazz Ensembles
description: Jazz Ensemble II, the Maryland Parkway Jazz Vocal Ensemble, and the Contemporary Jazz Ensemble perform on the third night of UNLV's spring jazz festival.

name: Guided Meditation Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Pacific Standard.

name: UNLV Dance: CFACoLab - That Show About the Hot Dog (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on November 11, 2025 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026.

name: UNLV Dance: Elegant Universes (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on April 28, 2026 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026. Elegant Universes is a polished showcase of new dance works created by UNLV BFA students in performance & choreography and production & management.

name: UNLV Dance: Gravitational Pulls (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on November 18, 2025 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026. Gravitational Pulls features captivating new dance works created by UNLV BFA students in performance & choreography and production & management.

name: UNLV Dance: Orbits (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on October 7, 2025 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026. Orbits is a celestial journey showcasing new works by the esteemed faculty and guest artists of UNLV Dance (including a traditional Georgian dance by choreographer Irakli Rusia) and featuring talented UNLV Dance BFA students.

name: UNLV Dance: Universal Motions (Streaming)
description: Streaming, recorded performance This video will be available from 7:30 p.m. on March 3, 2026 until 7:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026. Universal Motions features choreography by the UNLV Dance faculty, including a reconstruction of a work by 20th century American choreographer, Erick Hawkins.

name: 2026 Best Teaching Practices Expo
location:
Student Union
        Rooms 208 A,B, and C.
description: The UNLV Teaching & Learning Commons invites faculty and graduate students to the annual Best Teaching Practices Expo, featuring posters that showcase evidence-based teaching strategies developed by UNLV faculty. The event includes a Distinguished Posters Panel, selected by the Expo Review Committee, from 11:30 a.m. to 1 p.m. and features faculty with their students. This year, we will also introduce a People’s Choice Award, giving all attendees the opportunity to vote for their favorite poster. Brunch will be served beginning at 10 a.m.

name: A Day at the Library
location:
West Las Vegas Library
1861 N. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd
Las Vegas, NV 89106
description: "A Day at the Library," a collaborative project created by UNLV Department of Teaching and Learning students and local artists, is exhibiting at West Las Vegas Library from April 30 to July 12, 2026. A reception is planned from 1-2:30 p.m. on June 7.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: Countdown to Commencement Wristband & Swag Pick Up
location:
Richard Tam Alumni Center
        Tam Alumni Center and Registrar's Office
description: Stop by the Tam Alumni Center or the Registrar’s Office to pick up your Countdown to Commencement wristband and exclusive swag! Your wristband will give you access to special events and deals happening throughout the week.

name: APIME Mini Con
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Auditorium
description: Join us for an interactive experience exploring APIME communities, identity, and social impact. What to Expect Session 1: Creative placemaking and community building (Dr. Christina Ong) Session 2: Understanding and challenging the Model Minority Myth (Dr. Marijo Villano) Opportunities for dialogue, learning, and reflection. Attendees are welcome to participate in one or both sessions. Held in partnership with Barrick Museum

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Community in Action: Food Pantry Helper
description: Come out and give love at one of our last service events for the spring semester! On Thursday, April 30, meet us on the third floor of the Student Union in Room 316 at 12 p.m. After food and conversation, we will head out to The Henderson Equality Center to help stuff food bags and organize new stock deliveries for their pantry!

name: Understanding HRA Accounts
description: HSA Bank, the administrator for both HSAs and HRAs, is offering educational webinars designed specifically for the High Deductible health care participants. Each session provides a clear explanation of the different account type, how they work, and practical tips to help you get the most value from your benefits.

name: Understanding HSA Accounts
description: HSA Bank, the administrator for both HSAs and HRAs, is offering educational webinars designed specifically for the High Deductible health care participants. Each session provides a clear explanation of the different account type, how they work, and practical tips to help you get the most value from your benefits.

name: 2026 Rebel Career Champions Network (RCCN) Career Showcase
description: The 2026 Rebel Career Champions Network (RCCN) Career Showcase highlights the many ways UNLV faculty, staff, and programs make career readiness visible, intentional, and impactful for students. The event celebrates the innovative work happening across campus to prepare Rebels for success after graduation.

name: AANHPI Culture Night hosted by UNLV ROAR
description: Join UNLV ROAR (Raising Our AANHPI Rights) for AANHPI Culture Night, a celebration of the diversity, traditions, and contributions of Asian American, Native Hawaiian, and Pacific Islander communities. Attendees can connect with AANHPI student organizations, enjoy cultural performances, participate in interactive games, and take part in giveaways. Cultural attire is encouraged.

name: Global Thursday: Language Exchange
description: Interested in learning or practicing a new language? Do you love exploring language-culture connections? Join us every second Global Thursday for the Language Exchange Program (LEP) where students can explore languages such as Chinese, German, Vietnamese, Persian, Japanese, and more in a relaxed environment. No prior study required!

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Basketball (A)
description: Hit the court for a fast-paced, high-energy competition that combines skill, teamwork, and strategy! In the Basketball Challenge, teams pass, dribble, and shoot their way to score points while defending against their opponents.

name: Chicken N Pickle Event
location:
Chicken N' Pickle 
3381 St. Rose Pkwy
Henderson, NV, 89052
description: Graduates, join us at Chicken N Pickle to celebrate your accomplishments and connect with alumni clubs and chapters. Food and non-alcoholic beverages will be provided, and alcoholic beverages will be available for purchase. RSVP

name: UNLV/CSN Transfer Program (UCTP) Workshop
location:
CSN North Las Vegas Campus - Student Union
3200 East Cheyenne Ave.
North Las Vegas, NV 89030
description: Join us at our upcoming UNLV/CSN Transfer Program (UCTP) workshop! The transfer workshop will cover the transfer process, application deadlines, campus resources, upcoming UNLV events, and how credits earned at CSN will transfer to UNLV. We will highlight important transfer information for the different UNLV majors. This workshop is an opportunity to meet a UNLV transition advisor and address any transfer questions. Our in-person workshop will also have a credit review.

name: Mindfulness Brief Practice
description: ComPsych Huddle Practices are for those interested in joining a 25-minute guided practice, such as relaxation exercises, mindfulness meditation or stretching, to promote self-care moments during the day. Please note that all times are in Pacific Standard.

name: Nevada Conservatory Theatre: Giants Have Us in Their Books (Pay-What-You-Can Preview)
description: Six Children’s Plays for Adults by José Rivera Master storyteller José Rivera was inspired by his young daughter when she asked: if we have giants in our fairy tales, do giants have people in theirs? These metaphorical and poetic fairy tales for adults brim with magical realism, overflow with imagination, and radiate with contemporary relevance making for a soulful evening of theatre.

name: UNLV Opera Theater: Opera Workshop I - Organic Chemistry
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Recital Hall
description: UNLV Opera Theater presents scenes from opera and operetta about romantic and platonic chemistry.

Richard Tam Alumni Center

description: Stop by the Tam Alumni Center or the Registrar’s Office to pick up your Countdown to Commencement wristband and exclusive swag! Your wristband will give you access to special events and deals happening throughout the week.

name: UNLV Dance - Placement & BFA Audition - May 1, 2026
description: 10-11:30 a.m: Placement Auditions Placement auditions determine level placement for technique courses. Required for dance majors and non-majors looking to place into technique courses higher than the 100 level. Attendees will be assessed in technique in ballet, jazz, and modern. 12-1:30 p.m.: BFA Auditions

name: What to Expect During Open Enrollment Webinar
description: This webinar will discuss how open enrollment works and what changes you can make during open enrollment.

name: Department of Economics Seminar Series: Frank Fossen, University of Nevada, Reno
description: The Department of Economics would like to invite you to a seminar by Frank Fossen from the University of Nevada, Reno, where he is a professor of economics and the Charles N. Mathewson Professor in Entrepreneurship.

name: UNLV AI and Big Data iRDA Symposium Series: Biology and Health
description: This symposium will share AI-related research activities and discuss potential research collaborations specialized in biology and health. We welcome faculty, postdoc, graduate/undergraduate students who are interested in AI-related research in biology and health.

name: Senior Sing-Off
description: Join UNLVolunteers on May 1 from noon - 4 p.m. as we prepare for a day to hang out and sing with the senior folks of Quail House, all at the same time, while learning about how important relationships and networks are in helping combat homelessness. We will meet at the Student Union third floor conference room 316 inside the office of Service, Learning, & Leadership at 12 p.m., we will provide transportation for up to 10 people. We will be back by around 4 p.m. Bring your SLL punch card to reach the Owala Bottle prize if you have one.

name: Friday Flows: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. This 15-minute virtual session is designed to release tension from sitting, improve flexibility, and help you transition into your weekend feeling relaxed and refreshed. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: MSQF Finance Seminar Series: Vincent Bogousslavsky - Boston College
description: Vincent Bogousslavsky is an associate professor of finance and a Hillenbrand Family Faculty Fellow at Boston College. His research interests are in asset pricing and market microstructure.

name: First Sustainability in Arid Lands iRDA Symposium
description: Attend the 1st

name: UNLV Cannabis Policy Institute Third Annual Gaming & Cannabis Policy Discussion
description: UNLV’s Cannabis Policy Institute (CPI) and International Gaming Institute (IGI) will host the third annual Gaming & Cannabis Policy Discussion in the William S. Boyd School of Law’s Moot Courtroom as well as online via Zoom.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Building Tour - Kerkorian Medical Education Building
description: In 2022, the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV opened its new educational facility in the Las Vegas Medical District. The Office of Admissions is now offering a building tour program for those considering medical education (MD) degree studies.

name: Graduate MENTORS End-of-Program Commencement Event
description: Join us as we celebrate the successful completion of this year’s Graduate MENTORS Program! This culminating event honors the growth, connections, and achievements of our mentors and mentees throughout the program. Come reflect on the journey, share your experiences, and recognize the contributions of all participants. The event will feature brief remarks from program staff, mentor/mentee highlights, and opportunities to connect with fellow graduate students one last time before we wrap up this year's cohort.

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: American Red Cross Lifeguarding Certification Course
description: The American Red Cross Lifeguarding classes are designed to teach you not only the basics in water safety but also the proper protocol when dealing with any emergencies around water. The Red Cross blends both traditional lecture style and crucial hands-on training in order to teach you effective techniques to deliver prompt care to those in need. After successful completion of the course, participants will be certified in lifeguarding, CPR for the professional rescuer, AED, and first aid.

name: Nevada Conservatory Theatre: Giants Have Us in Their Books
description: Six Children’s Plays for Adults by José Rivera Master storyteller José Rivera was inspired by his young daughter when she asked: if we have giants in our fairy tales, do giants have people in theirs? These metaphorical and poetic fairy tales for adults brim with magical realism, overflow with imagination, and radiate with contemporary relevance making for a soulful evening of theatre.

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: HSI Resource Hub ​M​ay 2026 Webinar: Community Engaged Learning (CEL) in STEM at the University of Arizona with Dr. Kate Ellingson & Priscila Ruedas
description: Community Engaged Learning (CEL) in STEM provides a framework for connecting academic training with real-world, place-based challenges. This seminar presents the Border Health Service-Learning Institute (BHSLI), a graduate-level course at the University of Arizona that integrates CEL within a U.S.–Mexico border context.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Chess (A)
description: Engage your mind and strategy in this classic game of skill and tactics! The Chess Challenge invites participants to think several moves ahead, anticipate opponents’ strategies, and make calculated decisions to achieve checkmate.

name: Nevada Conservatory Theatre: Giants Have Us in Their Books - CANCELED
description: Due to an unforeseen power issue, the May 2, 2 p.m. performance has been canceled. The UNLV Performing Arts Center Box Office can help patrons with exchanges to another performance, or if necessary, a refund or donation of funds to the Nevada Conservatory Theatre. Six Children’s Plays for Adults by José Rivera

name: Study, Sip & Socialize with Comadres
location:
Servehzah Bottle Shop & Tap Room
1301 S Commerce St #130, Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: Join us for our end-of-semester writing mixer: a space to spark ideas, build momentum, and connect with fellow comadres while making progress on your work. Whether you’re drafting seminar papers, prepping for comps, revising articles, or working on your thesis or dissertation, bring your goals and settle in for focused writing in good company. Mix, mingle, write, and encourage one another as we navigate graduate school together. Come for the productivity, stay for the comunidad.

name: Jahun Koo Doctor of Musical Arts Recital in Jazz Piano
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Jazz Studio, room HFA 238 (second floor)
description: Jahun Koo presents a doctor of musical arts recital featuring a selection of jazz standards and an original composition. The program includes works by Bronisław Kaper, Toots Thielemans, Lee Morgan, Guy Wood, Richard Rodgers, Benny Golson, Luiz Bonfá, and Gene de Paul, along with an original work by the performer. The performance will feature a small jazz ensemble and will explore a variety of styles, including swing, Latin, and ballad forms, highlighting both traditional repertoire and personal artistic expression.

name: Nevada Conservatory Theatre: Giants Have Us in Their Books
description: Six Children’s Plays for Adults by José Rivera Master storyteller José Rivera was inspired by his young daughter when she asked: if we have giants in our fairy tales, do giants have people in theirs? These metaphorical and poetic fairy tales for adults brim with magical realism, overflow with imagination, and radiate with contemporary relevance making for a soulful evening of theatre.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Pickleball (A)
description: Step onto the court for a fun, fast-paced, and accessible game that combines elements of tennis, badminton, and ping-pong! In the Pickleball Challenge, teams compete in singles or doubles matches, aiming to score points by strategically placing shots and outmaneuvering their opponents.

name: Learn the Ropes: Climbing Edition
description: Are you a new or experienced climber looking for a beginner-friendly outdoor climbing experience? Join Outdoor Adventures for your first outdoor climb! This trip includes an optional “Gym to Crag” session that will teach you all the essential skills you need before climbing outside—such as setting anchors, tying knots, and managing a crag environment. The trip includes equipment, instruction, transportation, and food.

Las Vegas, NV 89106

name: Family Swim at SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during this time. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: UNLV Symphony Orchestra: Season Finale!
description: The UNLV Symphony Orchestra, conducted by the orchestra's artistic director Taras Krysa, performs Aaron Bruckner's Symphony No. 4.

name: Y.L. Douglas presents "The Learning Curve" Educator and Musician Prospective
location:
Alta Ham Fine Arts
        Room 238, Jazz Studio (Second Floor)
description: It has been a great learning curve entering into the Doctor of Musical Arts Program of Jazz and Commercial Music. I would like to share my experience in my first year featuring the Joe Williams Combo. Performers include: Jason Knuckles on saxophone, pianist Reggie "RJ" Johnson, Edoardo Lacala on bass, vocalist Isaiah Misailegalu, and Yolandus Y.L. Douglas on drums and percussion.

name: Arsenal Duo Piano and Pipe Organ Concert
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center
        Dr. Arturo Rando-Grillot Hall
description: Musicians Nathan Carterette, piano, and Edward Alan Moore, pipe organ, have joined forces as The Arsenal Duo, presenting concerts that unite their respective instruments in a unique and spectacular fashion. They draw from a diverse repertoire, including works originally for piano and orchestra; works original to piano and organ; their own transcriptions; and solo offerings.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Axe Throwing (A)
location:
Axehole Vegas
450 Fremont St Suite 163
Las Vegas, NV 89101
description: Step up, aim carefully, and test your precision in this thrilling and unique competition! The Axe Throwing Challenge invites participants to throw axes at a target, scoring points for accuracy while demonstrating focus, control, and technique.

name: Study Week Begins
description: Study week begins. Classes are scheduled, but major written exams are not given (with exception of mid-semester classes).

name: Lofi Study Lounge
description: Are you in search of the perfect ambiance to boost your focus during study week? Look no further! Join us at the Lied Library for a serene and productive study session at the Lofi Study Lounge. Study individually or with a group, all students welcome! Coffee, tea, and snacks provided!

name: Lofi Study Lounge
description: Are you in search of the perfect ambiance to boost your focus during study week? Look no further! Join us at the Lied Library for a serene and productive study session at the Lofi Study Lounge. Study individually or with a group–all students welcome. Coffee, tea, and snacks will be provided.

name: APIME-FSA End-of-Year Celebration
location:
Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
        Room 101
description: Join us at the APIME-FSA End-of-Year Celebration event to network with current/new members and learn how to get more involved. We will be serving lunch and giving out swag.

name: Junk Journaling
description: Join us in the makerspace for junk journaling, a creative and therapeutic practice that involves using scraps, discarded materials, or "junk" to create a journal. Learn about different way you can build and have fun with your journal. Materials will be provided. No orientation is required to attend this workshop, but if you haven't done one yet we encourage you to attend one to learn more about our space and become a registered user.

name: Non-Tenure Track Gathering (NTTG): How Can NTT Faculty Obtain Faculty Leave?
description: This month's topic: How can NTT faculty obtain faculty leave? Assistant professor in residence, Dave Nourse, Ph.D., who is a former chair of and current member of the Faculty Leave Committee, will join us for a discussion about faculty development leave and sabbatical for NTT faculty. If you were wanting to apply for leave and had questions about the process and what types of projects or research would qualify for and be awarded leave, please attend one of our two online sessions

name: Monday Moves: Functional Fitness at Your Desk
description: Start your week with purpose and energy right at your desk! This 15-minute session focuses on low-impact, functional movements designed to wake up your body, improve posture, and build strength without breaking a sweat. No need for workout gear or a mat, just login. These moves are perfect for refreshing your body and mind in the middle of the workday. What to Expect A short, energizing break from sitting

location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Cinco de Mayo Fiesta Walk
location:
The Flashlight
        Meet at The Flashlight by 9:50 a.m.
description: Join the UNLV Women's Council for a guided tour to enjoy the warming weather and the beautiful landscaping on campus. The tour will be led by the Landscape, Grounds, and Arboretum Department. Our group may discuss answers to questions such as "What's that called?" or "Where do plants go during construction projects?"

name: Non-Tenure Track Gathering (NTTG): How Can NTT Faculty Obtain Faculty Leave?
description: This month's topic: How can NTT faculty obtain faculty leave? Assistant professor in residence, Dave Nourse, Ph.D., who is a former chair of and current member of the Faculty Leave Committee, will join us for a discussion about faculty development leave and sabbatical for NTT faculty. If you were wanting to apply for leave and had questions about the process and what types of projects or research would qualify for and be awarded leave, please attend one of our two online sessions

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
location:
Frank and Estella Beam Hall
        Room 235 (Faculty Lounge)
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Buddy! Lock in. Can't lock in?
description: De-stress and craft a friend! Join us for a cozy crafting session where you’ll build a buddy, handmade companion to keep you company while you study. Take a break, get your hands moving, and leave with a pocket-sized pal to cheer you on through every study session.

name: Grounding and Centering Breath
description: Take 15 minutes with Anne Weisman on Tuesdays at noon to pause, breathe, and reset. This quick virtual session helps faculty and staff refocus, reduce stress, and recharge for the day.

name: Yoga Fitness Class
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        SRWC Meeting Room 1010
description: Take a mindful mid-day break with our 30-minute lunch time yoga series, designed specifically for UNLV faculty and staff. These sessions offer a refreshing blend of gentle movement, stretching, and breathwork to help you reset, reduce stress, and return to your day with renewed focus and energy. No experience is necessary—just bring a yoga mat, wear comfortable clothing, and come as you are. ﻿ ﻿Step away from your desk and join us for a well-deserved pause. Your mind and body will thank you!

name: Dissertation Defense: Amber Goto
description: Amber Goto, Ph.D Candidate Department of Psychology Exploring The Inner Experience of Individuals with Obsessive-Compulsive Symptoms Advisory Committee Members Russell Hurlburt, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Shane Kraus, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Nicole Short, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Sara Jordan, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: Dissertation Defense: Yangpeng Zhang
location:
Carol C. Harter Classroom Building Complex - Building C
        Room 212
description: Yangpeng Zhang, Ph.D Candidate Department of Mathematical Sciences Isogeometric Analysis For Maxwell’s Equations And An Memory-Efficient Algorithm For Time-Fractional PDEs Advisory Committee Members Jichun Li, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Pengtao Sun, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Monika Neda, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Hongtao Yang, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Yi-Tung Chen, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: Spring 2026 Senior Design Competition
description: The UNLV College of Engineering is proud to host the Spring 2026 Senior Design Competition, a long-standing capstone tradition that brings together the next generation of engineering innovators. This event challenges graduating seniors to design, develop, and present commercially viable projects and prototypes to a panel of industry experts.

name: Benefits Q&A Drop-In Session
description: Join our Virtual Open Enrollment Support Session and make confident decisions about your benefits! Get answers to your benefits-related questions Explore your plan options in a clear, guided way Speak one-on-one in private breakout rooms for personal or sensitive topics Whether you're reviewing your coverage or enrolling for the first time, support is available every step of the way.

name: AI and Meaningful Classroom Assessment
description: How has AI changed what you consider essential skills and learning outcomes in your field? How are you balancing assessment of final products versus the learning process? What’s the biggest pain point in having to re-think your assessments? These are some of the questions we and a faculty panel of your peers will be tackling at our May AI sessions. Join us at one (or both) of our sessions to hear from your colleagues across disciplines and identify future AI & assessment learning needs. Each session will feature a different panel of AI Fellows and faculty.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: Sound Bath & Soul Movement
description: Join us for a session that harnesses the power of sound healing, mindful movements, and intentional breathing to harmonize the mind, body, and soul.

name: Lavender (LGBTQIA+) Celebration of Student Success
description: Join us for a special evening as we celebrate you, your hard work, resilience, and the journey that brought you to this moment. This celebration honors LGBTQIA+ graduating students and the incredible milestones they have achieved during their time at UNLV. Come together with friends, family, faculty, and staff as we celebrate in joy and community while recognizing your accomplishments and looking ahead to all that comes next.

name: Monarch Celebration of Student Success
description: We proudly honor the accomplishments of our graduating students through our celebrations, held exclusively during the spring semester. These celebrations recognize the unique and valuable contributions of students representing various communities.

name: REB General Member Meetings
description: Join REB for our general member meetings to help plan our future events!

name: College of Education Alumni Campus Tour
location:
Cottage Grove Parking Garage
        Park in the Cottage Grove Garage (charges may apply)
Meet at the benches at the southeast exit of the garage
description: When was the last time you visited UNLV? College of Education alumni are invited back to campus for a custom 60-minute campus golf cart tour. Reminisce about the time you spent here for your studies and activities and check out the newest buildings being developed. Stops at Carlson Education Building, Tam Alumni Center, and the UNLV Bookstore included. Each party will receive a complimentary goodie bag. Space is limited. Up to five attendees per tour allowed.

location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        112
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response. Audience

name: Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Workshop
description: Feeling a little confused about Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) and what it means for your financial aid? No worries, you're not alone! Come to "From Criteria to Clarity: Navigating SAP Standards and the Appeal Process" webinar. We'll break down the SAP policy for keeping your financial aid in simple terms. Plus, we'll show you exactly what to do if you ever need to appeal. Get the info you need to stay on track and keep your aid flowing!

name: Study Abroad In-Person Informational Session
description: Dreaming of exploring the world while earning your degree? The chance to study abroad is calling—and it's closer than you think! Join our in person informational session to learn how we can guide you through every step of your adventure.

name: Paws for a Study Break
description: Take a study break in Lied Library with furry friends who are excited for pets, cuddles, and selfies. Therapy dogs will be joining us from Pet Partners of Las Vegas and Love Dog Adventures.

name: Writing Essentials Wednesdays
description: This semester, join the Writing Center on the first Wednesday of each month at 1 p.m. to strengthen your writing habits and skills. This spring, we'll be focusing on starting out strong. Have a paper due soon that you haven't yet begun? Were you just assigned an essay for class? Attend this workshop to learn strategies to set yourself up for success. We'll touch on topics such as turning your assignment prompt into a helpful checklist; organizing your ideas; and citing your sources.

name: Teaching and Learning Innovation Faculty Drop-In Hours
description: Join us for flexible, walk-in support designed for instructors of all modalities. Whether you are teaching online, hybrid, or face-to-face, our experts from UNLV Online, the Teaching & Learning Commons (TLC), and IT are here to help you refine your craft and troubleshoot your tech. This semester, we are placing a special focus on effective and ethical uses of AI in teaching, offering practical guidance on how to integrate AI tools like NotebookLM and ChatGPT into your curriculum.

name: Virtual: Alternative Route to Licensure (Traditional) Information Session
description: This information session is specifically for our traditional ARL program and does not cover the Accelerated Graduate Apprenticeship program. For details on the next cohort cycle of the Graduate Apprenticeship program (formerly known as A-ARL), please visit the program's website.

name: Jiu Jitsu For Self-Defense
description: Join us for a Jiu-Jitsu class. This a grappling-based martial art and self-defense system with four main components: taking your opponent to the ground, passing their legs to gain a dominant position, pinning your opponent, and submitting them through various joint locks and chokes. Jiu-Jitsu emphasizes leverage, body mechanics, and technique, not necessarily strength, although it helps. Participants will learn various techniques to defend themselves against larger, stronger, and untrained opponents. All ages and skill levels are encouraged.

name: Lance & Elena Calvert Awards for Undergraduate Research Ceremony
description: Celebrate the winners of the 2026 Lance and Elena Calvert Awards for Undergraduate Research. Refreshments will be provided.

name: UNLV Toastmasters Open House
description: Do you need to sharpen your leadership and presentation skills? Join us at Toastmasters and see how improving your ability to communicate will bring out the inner leader in you!

name: Mid-Week Dinner
description: Gather with fellow international students every Wednesday at the Dining Commons for dinner and camaraderie. Open to all the UNLV community interested in building friendships and sharing cultural experiences.

name: 2026 Corporate Challenge: Badminton (A)
location:
Dula Gym
451 E Bonanza Rd.
Las Vegas, NV 89101
description: Step onto the court for a fast-paced, skillful, and energetic competition! The Badminton Challenge tests reflexes, precision, and strategy as teams or individuals volley the shuttlecock back and forth, aiming to score points while outmaneuvering their opponents.

name: Asian, Pacific Islander & Middle Eastern (APIME) Celebration of Student Success
description: Join us for a special evening as we celebrate you — your hard work, resilience, and the journey that brought you to this moment. Let’s come together in joy and community to honor your accomplishments and look ahead to all that’s next! This celebration is open to all students, faculty, and staff — feel free to bring your friends, family, and supporters (limit of 4 guests per graduating student). Each registered graduating student will be presented with a special stole during the celebration.

name: Spring 2026 Senior Design Awards Ceremony
location:
Thomas and Mack Center
        Thomas and Mack Pavilion
description: The UNLV College of Engineering proudly celebrates the Spring 2026 Senior Design Competition winners with a special awards ceremony. This biannual tradition honors Senior Design teams that earned first‑ and second‑place prizes across discipline‑specific categories, along with additional awards recognizing top projects in commercial viability and sustainability. Winners in the Innovation, Popular Choice, Team Congeniality, and Grand Prize categories will also be announced.

name: Science Cafe - Speaker Cody Colleps, Ph.D.
location:
McMullan's Irish Pub
4650 W Tropicana Ave.
description: Science Cafe is held the first Wednesday of every month at McMullan's Irish Pub (4650 W. Tropicana Ave). Doors open at 6:30 p.m. with trivia and the presentation starting promptly at 7 p.m. Bring friends and family for informal public discussions with leading scientists on interesting topics. Grab some dinner while listening. Cody Colleps, Ph.D., an assistant professor in UNLV's Department of Geoscience will be giving a talk titled, "A Rock’s Tale Told by the Noblest of Gases: How Helium in Minerals can Constrain Rates and Dates of Geological Processes".

name: Benefits Q&A Drop-In Session
description: Join our Virtual Open Enrollment Support Session and make confident decisions about your benefits! Get answers to your benefits-related questions Explore your plan options in a clear, guided way Speak one-on-one in private breakout rooms for personal or sensitive topics Whether you're reviewing your coverage or enrolling for the first time, support is available every step of the way.

name: Breathing Break - Hosted by UNLV PRACTICE
description: Give yourself a little pause at 10 a.m. on Thursdays with a 15-minute guided breathing session. It is a chance to relax, reset, and bring a little calm to your day.

name: Mindful Moments: Weekly Guided Meditation
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: The Impact of Attitude on Work and Life
description: Overview/ Description: While you obviously need the appropriate knowledge and skills to do your job, the mindset with which you approach your work plays a dominant role in your ability to perform. If you’re discouraged, you work sluggishly, think small and give up easily. However, when you feel good, confident and exhilarated you can win people to your side and create dazzling projects. This workshop will help participants explore how attitude can change their life and will provide them with opportunities to develop a more positive mindset.

location:

        326

name: Narcan Training
location:
Student Recreation & Wellness Center
        Health Education Room 1502, 1st Floor of Student Recreation & Wellness Center
description: Join us for a Narcan Training focused on opioid overdose awareness and response. Participants will learn what opioids are, how to recognize the signs of an overdose, and the steps to safely intervene and help save a life if ever needed. This training centers on harm reduction, community care, and building confidence to respond in an emergency. Attendees will receive free Narcan, a CPR mask, and a first aid kit to take home. Chick-fil-A will be provided while supplies last on a first-come, first-served basis.

name: Tianran Gao Doctor of Musical Arts Piano Recital: Scriabin, Schubert, Liszt

name: Play Along At Home: Piloting a Model for Home-School-Media Partnerships
description: It is often said that parents are a child’s first teacher. In recognition of this truth, Vegas PBS developed a pilot program to strengthen science and socioemotional learning for Las Vegas preschoolers by bridging home, school, and media environments as interconnected learning spaces.

location:

description: Slide into finals with this fantastic end of the year pool party! Enjoy a slushie and relax in the leisure pool on an inner tube. Party with your friends on the pool's slide. description: Close out your week with a calming, feel-good stretch break right from the comfort of your desk. No equipment or change of clothes needed, just join in, breathe deeply, and let go of the week’s stress one stretch at a time.

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: LaVell Thompson and Friends: Concert of Original Works
location:
Lee and Thomas Beam Music Center

description: Please join doctor of musical arts candidate LaVell Thompson, Jr., as he presents an hour's worth of music. All of the compositions and arrangements featured in this concert will be original works composed by him! There will be a world premiere of a string quartet, two song cycles, as well as a chamber orchestral piece.

name: Annual Open Enrollment & RebelWELL Wellness Health Fair
location:
Student Union
        Room 208 and Nearby Breakout Rooms
description: UNLV Human Resources and Wellness would love to invite you to the Annual Open Enrollment & RebelWELL Wellness Health Fair, a welcoming campus event designed to support your well-being and help you feel informed, connected, and supported. At the fair, you'll be able to connect with benefits providers, campus partners, and wellness organizations, with many opportunities to ask questions, explore available resources, and learn more about programs that support your health, well-being, and work life balance on this special day.

name: Graduation Cap Decorating

All you need to bring is your graduation cap and some creativity! Leave with a decorated cap to celebrate your big day.

name: Open Enrollment & RebelWELL Wellness Fair - Financial Wellness
description: Rebel Budget - Let us assist with determining your budget for your home purchase. We have a promo special.

name: Open Enrollment & RebelWELL Wellness Fair - Gratitude & Positivity Reflection Room
description: Step into a calm, reflective space designed to reset your mindset and reconnect with what matters most. This self-guided experience invites you to pause, reflect, and focus on moments of gratitude and positivity in your life. Soft meditation music and a welcoming environment create the space for you to write down something good. Whether it is a recent win, a moment of kindness, or something you are grateful for, your message becomes part of a shared experience that uplifts the UNLV community. Take a moment. Share something good. Leave feeling lighter and more grounded.

name: Open Enrollment & RebelWELL Wellness Fair - Posture and Back Safety in the Workplace
description: A practical session focused on physical wellness, including strategies to improve posture and reduce back strain in the workplace.

name: Open Enrollment & RebelWELL Wellness Fair - Whole-Person Learning: Enrichment, Belonging, and Workforce Growth
description: This session highlights how lifelong learning supports personal enrichment, connection, and overall well being, while strengthening career mobility and occupational wellness.

name: Open Enrollment & RebelWELL Wellness Fair – Money Psychology: Beyond the Numbers
description: It’s easy to think that how you approach money is all about formulas, facts and figures. The truth is, decisions are influenced by biases, life experiences, and human nature. This session explores those forces and how to use them to your advantage.

name: Mastering the Moment: Preparing for Commencement (Virtual Session)
description: For students who couldn’t attend the in-person Mastering the Moment session, we’re excited to offer a virtual workshop to help you prepare for your upcoming commencement ceremony. This online event, hosted via Zoom, will cover everything you need to know to confidently navigate your graduation day and celebrate this significant academic milestone.

description: This session explores how chronic stress impacts emotional regulation and relationships. Participants will learn techniques for de escalating emotional moments and using empathetic listening to repair relationships.

name: Virtual: Master of Science in Hotel Administration Information Session
description: Interested in hotel administration? Join us to learn more about what the Master of Hotel Administration at UNLV has to offer! The 31-hour Master of Science in Hotel Administration degree program will prepare you for a successful career as an upper-level executive in the hospitality industry. The typical completion time for full-time students is 1 year; part-time students usually take 2 years to complete the program.

name: BSN Program Virtual Information Session
description: UNLV Nursing welcomes students interested in pursuing a career in nursing to join a virtual informational session on May 13 from 11 a.m. to 12 p.m. to learn more about the pre-licensure program. This session will outline the pathway from pre-requisite work to admission to the School of Nursing program, provide an overview of the program, courses, clinical rotations, and resources, and include support from student services, the Student Nurse Association, and more. Prospective students will also have the opportunity to ask questions directly to the program directors.

name: UNLV Graduate College Doctoral Recognition Event
description: Join the Graduate College as we celebrate the remarkable achievements of UNLV's spring 2026 doctoral graduates at our Doctoral Recognition event! This event honors the inspiring individuals who have navigated this path and reached the pinnacle of academia.

Las Vegas, NV 89120
description: Step onto the ice for a strategic and team-focused competition that blends precision, communication, and finesse! description: Angelos Tsalafos, Ph.D. Candidate Department of Psychology Validation of the Brief Resilience Scale and Prediction of Psychological Distress from Resilience and Performance Optimization in College Students Advisory Committee Members: Bradley Donohue, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Chair Shane Kraus, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member Joanne Ullman, Ph.D., Advisory Committee Member John Mercer, Ph.D., Graduate College Representative

name: Native American Celebration of Student Success
description: Join us for a special evening as we celebrate you — your hard work, resilience, and the journey that brought you to this moment. Let’s come together in joy and community to honor your accomplishments and look ahead to all that’s next! This celebration is open to all students, faculty, and staff — feel free to bring your friends, family, and supporters (limit of 4 guests per graduating student). Each registered graduating student will be presented with a special stole during the celebration.

name: Family Swim Fridays at the SRWC Pool
description: SRWC members may bring up to three guests free of charge during Family Swim days. Guests do not have to be members. Youth ages 6 months to 18 years are also allowed to swim at the pool during this time. We offer private and group swim lessons for youth ages 6 months to 18 years. Any guests who are not toilet-trained must wear a swim diaper. The member must be present in the water with their guests. Guests are required to stay in the pool area and are not permitted to use the rest of the facility.

name: Chamberlain University: Spring Graduation Ceremony
description: Chamberlain University celebrates its Bachelor of Science in Nursing graduates.

name: Spring 2026 Commencement: Graduate Degree Candidates
description: All those completing a graduate certificate, master's, specialists, and doctoral degree are invited to attend. The ceremony will begin at 4 p.m.

name: African Heritage Celebration of Student Success
description: We proudly honor the accomplishments of our graduating students through our celebrations, held exclusively during the spring semester. These celebrations recognize the unique and valuable contributions of students representing various communities.

name: Meet with a TIAA Representative on Campus
description: Meet with a TIAA financial consultant to help create a financial plan for your future. Discuss investments, saving and withdrawing from your plan when you retire. All employees are welcome to meet with a consultant.

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast - Tap & Ash
location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: Graduation Cap Decorating
description: Visit the Lied Library Makerspace to design and create your customized graduation caps. A broad array of free supplies will be available to help make your vision come true. All you need to bring is your graduation cap and some creativity! Leave with a decorated cap to celebrate your big day!

name: Spring 2026 Commencement: Undergraduate Degree Candidates
description: The spring 2026 commencement schedule for undergraduate degree candidates is as follows: 9 A.M. Ceremony • Fine Arts • Integrated Health Sciences • Liberal Arts • Nursing • Public Health • Urban Affairs 3 P.M. Ceremony • Business • Education • Engineering • Hospitality • Sciences

name: Willow Beach Stand Up Paddling Day
description: Kick off your summer with the ultimate beach day at Arizona's Willow Beach. Whether you're a total newbie or a seasoned pro, our trip leaders will have you gliding on a Stand Up Paddleboard (SUP) in no time. Dive into the crystal-clear waters of the Colorado River, soak up the desert sun, and hang out with the Outdoor Adventures crew on our most relaxing trip of the year! Transportation, instruction, gear, and lunch/snacks are included in the price.

name: 91.5 Jazz and More Broadcasting Live: Sunset Jazz Series - Jarez
location:
Side Lawn at Sunset Station
1301 W Sunset Rd, 
Henderson, NV 89014
description: JaRez is a Billboard-charting saxophonist, producer, and creative visionary who has redefined the modern smooth jazz landscape with a sound that fuses sophistication, soul, and streetwise cool. Known for blending futuristic grooves with emotional depth, Jarez continues to evolve—both musically and culturally—while shaping the future of contemporary jazz. 91.5 Jazz and More will be broadcasting live.

name: Live Broadcast from Vic's Supper Club
location:
Vic's Supper Club
355 Promenade Place
Las Vegas, NV 89106
description: 91.5 Jazz and More is at Vic's Supper Club in Symphony Park. Join us this Saturday night for a live broadcast featuring seasonal cocktails, an Italian menu and live jazz performed by national headliners and the Las Vegas Jazz Academy trio. Parking is validated, every Saturday at 8pm as 91.5 Jazz and More broadcast Live from Vic's Supper Club in Symphony Park.

name: Alt Breaks Spring Break Trip - Conservation & Ecology on Catalina Island, CA (apply by April 19))
description: Travel to Catalina Island Conservancy to learn more about ecology justice. This trip will contain various service projects that can range from trail maintenance to gardening, to seed collection. Participants will get a better understanding of social identity, social justice, racism, and other important topics and how they intersect with the topic of ecology justice.

name: UNLV College of Fine Arts: Graduation Ceremony
description: Students are welcome to attend both the university ceremony on May 15 and the college ceremony on May 17. The commencement ceremony held on May 15 in the Thomas & Mack Center includes graduates from six colleges in the university. The graduation ceremony on Sunday, May 17 celebrates only those students graduating from the College of Fine Arts (CFA). Because it is limited to the college, it will be a very intimate affair honoring CFA graduates; graduates will also receive special gifts. The college is also hooding M.Arch. and MFA candidates, along with DMAs.

name: Resiliency: Bouncing Back After a Setback
description: Overview/Description: Certain individuals appear to succeed against all odds. Despite hardships such as living through war, witnessing the death of a loved one, having a chronic illness, being a victim of abuse or dealing with unemployment, certain people not only adapt but actually thrive. These people possess a quality known as “resilience.” This workshop will examine the characteristics of resilience and discover ways to introduce greater resilience into our lives.

name: UNLV Launchpad: Navigating Your Career After Graduation
description: Are you a recent graduate ready to take the next step in your career and life design? UNLV Alumni Engagement and Career Services & Workforce Development teams are here to help you navigate your career journey after graduation. Join us for our UNLV Launchpad: Navigating Your Career After Graduation and connect with employers and alumni, get resume and interview tips, and effectively navigate your next move. Don't miss this chance to launch your successful career! This event is for you if:

name: Welcome to Your First Year at UNLV
description: Connect with the First Year Experience Team to chat and ask any questions about all things first year at UNLV!

name: APIME-FSA Mahjong Night
description: APIME-FSA members are invited to learn and play the iconic game of Mahjong while being in community with other faculty & staff. Light refreshments will be provided and space is limited, so RSVPs are encouraged.

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Admissions Information Session
description: These virtual information sessions are for individuals who are starting to consider medical school as a graduate degree option and career path. We welcome high school seniors, community college and university students, or other individuals just beginning to research medical school and/or beginning to plan for their application. We will offer an overview of the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV, resources to help consider medical school, details regarding application, and a Q&A at the end of the session.

name: Summer Camp: Literacy with the Arts
description: Three Days. Three Art Forms. One Unforgettable Experience. Spark your child’s creativity and confidence through a dynamic exploration of literacy and the arts. This unique camp blends poetry, visual art, and creative expression to help students discover their voice and bring their ideas to life. Throughout the experience, students will write and perform original poetry alongside a local artist, design and create their own public art pieces, and explore how artwork is curated and displayed in an exhibition setting.

name: UNLV Women's Council Women of Color Leaders at the Table Workshop
description: Please join the UNLV Women's Council for this engaging workshop to learn various paths of opportunity for women of color who aspire to be future leaders. Attendees will create an action plan to implement in their own career path while networking with fellow colleagues in this safe place.

name: Improving Health Outcomes for Intersex, Transgender, and Nonbinary Communities: From Awareness to Action
description: This training is designed to promote understanding of the unique and shared health experiences of intersex, transgender, and nonbinary communities, while encouraging meaningful engagement and action. Participants will learn to identify community health needs and build confidence in seeking and sharing accurate information to increase awareness and improve health outcomes.

name: Science Cafe - Pamela Burnley, Ph.D.
location: McMullan's Irish Pub, 4650 W Tropicana Ave.
description: Science Cafe is held the first Wednesday of every month at McMullan's Irish Pub (4650 W. Tropicana Ave). Doors open at 6:30 p.m. with trivia and the presentation starting promptly at 7 p.m. Bring friends and family for informal public discussions with leading scientists on interesting topics. Grab some dinner while listening. Pamela Burnley, Ph.D., a professor in UNLV's Department of Geoscience will be giving a talk titled, "How to Fold a Rock." Abstract:

name: Active Assailant 8-Hour Training
location:
CSN North Las Vegas Campus - Building P
        Tyrone Thompson Student Union - Room 126 & 128
description: The University Police Department is pleased to offer a dynamic 8-hour, in-person Active Assailant Training designed to provide hands-on experience and practical tools to help participants prepare for and respond to emergency situations. This comprehensive training will equip participants with: Critical response strategies for active threat situations Interactive, scenario-based exercises that simulate real-life emergencies Expert instruction from law enforcement professionals

description: Join us for the Intro to Pre-Professional Health workshop to explore careers in medicine, dentistry, optometry, veterinary medicine, and other professional health fields. Attending this workshop is required for all students before meeting with a pre-professional advisor.

name: Rewards and Challenges of the Blended Family
description: With more than half of all marriages ending in divorce, and the majority of divorced individuals finding new partners, the number of blended families is growing. This type of situation can create a range of challenging issues, both practical and emotional. Patience, understanding and open communication can help make the transition smoother for everyone. In this webinar, we will:

name: Opera Las Vegas: Leoncavallo's I Pagliacci
description: Ruggero Leoncavallo’s I Pagliacci is a landmark of the verismo movement, a one-act opera that blurs the boundary between theatrical performance and raw human emotion. Set in a rural Italian village, it tells the story of a traveling commedia dell’arte troupe whose offstage tensions spiral tragically into real life.

location:
Tap N Ash Social Club
1606 S Commerce St, Las Vegas, NV, USA
Las Vegas, NV 89102
description: 91.5 Jazz and More Live Broadcast Presents Cigars, Bourbon and Soul at Tap N Ash located in Downtown Las Vegas in the Arts District. Live Soul Friday Night! Local and national artist take the stage for a live on-air broadcast from 8 - 10 p.m. on a Friday night.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: UNLV NextGen Curators: Virtual Museum of Community, Identity & Creativity
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art
description: UNLV NextGen Curators: Virtual Museum of Community, Identity & Creativity is an immersive camp designed for high school students interested in art, technology, storytelling, and creative innovation. Hosted by the Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art in collaboration with faculty from UNLV Fine Arts, Engineering, and Communication Studies, this program will introduce students to the exciting world of digital museum curation and exhibition design.

1650 S. Pavilion Center Dr, Las Vegas, NV 89135

name: Canoe Emerald Cave
description: Ready to see the Colorado River like never before? Join Outdoor Adventures for a four-mile round-trip canoe journey into the heart of the desert. We are paddling straight toward the legendary Emerald Cove, where the water glows a neon green so bright you will think it is a filter. This bucket-list experience is perfect for beginners and seasoned paddlers, as our Trip Leaders teach you everything you need to know Transportation, instruction, gear, and lunch/snacks are included in the price.

name: Live Broadcast from Vic's Supper Club
location:
Vic's Supper Club
355 Promenade Place
Las Vegas, NV 89106
description: 91.5 Jazz and More is at Vic's Supper Club in Symphony Park. Join us this Saturday night for a live broadcast featuring seasonal cocktails, an Italian menu and live jazz performed by national headliners and the Las Vegas Jazz Academy trio. Parking is validated, every Saturday at 8pm as 91.5 Jazz and More broadcast Live from Vic's Supper Club in Symphony Park.

name: Circus Meets Medicine 2026 Conference
location:
Las Vegas City Hall
495 S Main St #150
Las Vegas, NV 89101
description: Circus Meet Medicine connects entertainment, medicine, academia, and research through live performance, presentations, networking, and more! The UNLV College of Fine and Performing Arts/Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine's Consortium for the Arts + Medicine presents the 4th annual Circus Meets Medicine medical conference! This is a one-of-a-kind medical conference featuring presentations from healthcare professionals working with the entertainment industry, live demonstrations and Q&A sessions with professional acrobats, networking opportunities, and a community panel.

name: Cupcake Canva
location:
Student Services Complex - Building C
        Rebels CU Branch
description: Looking for a sweet midday break? Whether you are faculty, staff, or a student worker, take a break from your desk, unleash your inner artist, and treat yourself to a DIY cupcake decorating event! While you design your masterpiece and enjoy your tasty cupcake, you can also explore all the sweet perks, services, and financial resources that Rebels CU—UNLV’s on-campus credit union—has to offer! Cupcakes are limited, so make sure to swing by early to secure your sweet treat! We can’t wait to see your delicious creations. See you there!

name: Signature Homes Down Payment Assistance Program Informational Session
description: Signature Homes and the Nevada Housing Division have launched a new affordable housing incentive program designed to make homeownership more attainable for Nevadans. The Down Payment Assistance Program offers qualified homebuyers up to $55,000 toward the purchase of a new home at Signature Homes’ Paradise Trails community, located near Desert Inn Rd. and Eastern Ave. — just 15 minutes from the Maryland Campus. The program includes:

name: Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV: Admissions Information Session
description: These virtual information sessions are for individuals who are starting to consider medical school as a graduate degree option and career path. We welcome high school seniors, community college and university students, or other individuals just beginning to research medical school and/or beginning to plan for their application. We will offer an overview of the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV, resources to help consider medical school, details regarding application, and a Q&A at the end of the session.

name: Art Exhibition: Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology
location:
Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art / Harry Reid Center
        Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art, East Gallery and WorkShop Gallery
description: The Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art is proud to present Actions for the Earth: Art, Care & Ecology, an exhibition curated by Sharmila Wood and produced by Independent Curators International (ICI) on view from Feb. 20–June 13, 2026. Actions for the Earth is a traveling exhibition that considers how artistic practices use kinship, healing, and restorative intervention to foster a deeper consciousness of our interconnectedness with the earth.

name: Intro to Pre-Law Workshop
description: Interested in a law career but not sure where to start? Come to the virtual Intro to Pre-Law workshop to find out more how to apply to law school. We will leave time for a 15 minute Q & A session after the completion of the workshop. Attending this workshop is required for 1st year students before meeting with a pre-professional advisor. All workshops are virtual and a link will be sent one to two days prior to the workshop to your UNLV email.

name: Chemical Hygiene and Laboratory Safety Training
description: This training course is designed to cover topics in general laboratory safety including personal protective equipment, the proper use of chemical fume hoods, hazardous chemical usage and storage, hazard communication, proper waste segregation and waste disposal, proper gas cylinder use, and spill and emergency response.

name: Prospective Undergraduate Engineering Spotlight Tour
description: Curious about studying engineering at UNLV? Join us at our Engineering Spotlight Tour! This specially designed event aims at aiding prospective undergraduate students and their families in learning about all Howard R. Hughes. College of Engineering has to offer.

name: Hands Only CPR Guinness World Records Record Attempt
description: West Henderson Hospital has teamed up with the Las Vegas Raiders for an exciting community initiative: an official Guinness World Records attempt for the “Most People Performing CPR in 12 Hours." West Henderson Hospital has invited affiliates of both UNLV and Nevada State University to participate in this incredible event, and they are currently seeking CPR-trained “Steward” volunteers. General volunteers are also welcome to participate.

name: Mummy's Nose Advanced Hike
description: Take your mountain game to the next level with Outdoor Adventures on our most challenging trek yet: the ascent of Mummy’s Nose. This isn't our typical hike; it’s an advanced alpine expedition that will push your limits as we tackle nearly 3,000 feet of elevation gain in just 2.5 miles. Prepare to navigate rugged limestone ridges, scramble through ancient Bristlecone Pine forests, and master a thrilling Class 3 rock chute as we reach the 10,751-foot summit. The reward is a breathtaking 360-degree panorama of the Mt. Charleston Wilderness that few ever get to see.

name: Information Session for Administrative Faculty Employees: Understanding UNLV’s Rebel Job Framework
description: This session introduces employees to the Rebel Job Framework, anticipated to launch for administrative employees in July 2026. The session will explain the purpose of the new job classification structure and its core components, providing employees with an understanding of how positions were classified and mapped within the framework.

name: Rebel Job Framework: Supervisors and Managers of Administrative Faculty Training
description: This virtual training equips managers and supervisors with the knowledge and tools needed to support their teams under the Rebel Job Framework. The sessions will outline the purpose and benefits of the new job classification structure, highlighting framework components such as the differences between job descriptions and position descriptions, career streams/levels, and work dimensions. Managers and supervisors will learn about how classification requests are submitted and reviewed, their responsibilities within the process, and the resources available to assist them.

name: Making the Most of Family Occasions
description: If you are a parent you probably share an almost universal desire to make family occasions special for your children. You also may have to overcome certain obstacles in creating your own special experiences with your children such as finances, distance from family members, and the special circumstances created by divorce, step-parenting and blended families. Many parents work overtime only to find themselves frustrated by the experience and doubtful that the end result was particularly meaningful to their children. In this webinar, we will:

name: UNLV/CSN Transfer Program (UCTP) Virtual Workshop
description: Join us at our upcoming UNLV/CSN Transfer Program (UCTP) VIRTUAL workshop! The transfer workshop will cover the transfer process, application deadlines, campus resources, upcoming UNLV events, and how credits earned at CSN will transfer to UNLV. We will highlight important transfer information for the different UNLV majors. This workshop is an opportunity to meet a UNLV transition advisor and address any transfer questions. Our virtual workshop will also have a credit review.

location:
Side Lawn at Sunset Station
1301 W. Sunset Road
Henderson, NV 89014
description: Saxophonist Adrian Crutchfield is known around the world for creating and performing music in a variety of genres such as jazz, funk, hip-hop, rock, and more. Adrian’s star began shining at the age of four when he received his first saxophone during a concert in Roanoke, VA from acclaimed jazz artist Kenny G; who noticed Adrian’s interest from the audience, stopped the show, and invited him to the stage, playing a childish melody, and finally bestowing the sax to young Adrian. 91.5 Jazz and More will be broadcasting live!
